title: Chief Executives
description: Determine and formulate policies and provide overall direction of enterprises or private and public sector organizations.

title: General and Operations Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the operations of public or private sector organizations.

title: Legislators
description: Develop, introduce, or enact laws and statutes at the local, tribal, state, or federal level.

title: Advertising and Promotions Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate advertising policies and programs or produce collateral materials.

title: Marketing Managers
description: Determine the demand for products and services and identify potential customers.

title: Sales Managers
description: Direct the actual distribution or movement of a product or service to the customer.

title: Public Relations Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities designed to create or maintain a favorable public image.

title: Fundraising Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities to solicit and maintain funds for special projects or nonprofit organizations.

title: Administrative Services Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate one or more administrative services of an organization.

title: Facilities Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the physical plant, grounds, or related equipment of an organization.

title: Security Managers
description: Direct an organization's security functions, including physical security and safety of employees.

title: Computer and Information Systems Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities in such fields as electronic data processing, information systems, and computer programming.

title: Financial Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate accounting, investing, banking, insurance, securities, and other financial activities.

title: Treasurers and Controllers
description: Direct financial activities, such as planning, procurement, and investments for all or part of an organization.

title: Investment Fund Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate investment strategy or operations for a large pool of liquid assets.

title: Industrial Production Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the work activities and resources necessary for manufacturing products.

title: Quality Control Systems Managers
description: Direct quality control programs to ensure continuous production of products consistent with established standards.

title: Purchasing Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the activities of buyers, purchasing officers, and related workers involved in purchasing materials.

title: Transportation, Storage, and Distribution Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate transportation, storage, or distribution activities in accordance with organizational policies.

title: Supply Chain Managers
description: Direct or coordinate production, purchasing, warehousing, distribution, or financial forecasting to limit costs and improve accuracy.

title: Compensation and Benefits Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate compensation and benefits activities of an organization.

title: Human Resources Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate human resources activities and staff of an organization.

title: Training and Development Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the training and development activities and staff of an organization.

title: Farmers, Ranchers, and Other Agricultural Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the management or operations of farms, ranches, greenhouses, and other agricultural establishments.

title: Construction Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate, usually through subordinate supervisory personnel, activities concerned with the construction and maintenance of structures.

title: Education and Childcare Administrators, Preschool and Daycare
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate academic or nonacademic activities of preschools or childcare centers.

title: Education Administrators, Kindergarten through Secondary
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the academic, administrative, or auxiliary activities of public or private elementary or secondary level schools.

title: Education Administrators, Postsecondary
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate research, instructional, student administration and services, and other educational activities at postsecondary institutions.

title: Architectural and Engineering Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities in such fields as architecture and engineering or research and development.

title: Food Service Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities of an organization or department that serves food and beverages.

title: Gambling Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate gambling operations in a casino.

title: Entertainment and Recreation Managers, Except Gambling
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of an establishment.

title: Lodging Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities of an organization or department that provides lodging and other accommodations.

title: Medical and Health Services Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate medical and health services in hospitals, clinics, managed care organizations, or similar organizations.

title: Natural Sciences Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities in such fields as life sciences, physical sciences, mathematics, statistics, and research and development.

title: Clinical Research Coordinators
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate clinical research projects. Direct the activities of workers engaged in clinical research projects.

title: Postmasters and Mail Superintendents
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate operational, administrative, management, and supportive services of a U.S. post office.

title: Property, Real Estate, and Community Association Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the selling, buying, leasing, or governance activities of commercial, industrial, or residential real estate properties.

title: Social and Community Service Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the activities of a social service program or community outreach organization.

title: Emergency Management Directors
description: Plan and direct disaster response or crisis management activities, provide disaster preparedness training, and prepare emergency plans.

title: Funeral Home Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate the services or resources of funeral homes.

title: Fitness and Wellness Coordinators
description: Manage or coordinate fitness and wellness programs and services.

title: Spa Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate activities of a spa facility.

title: Regulatory Affairs Managers
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate production activities of an organization to ensure compliance with regulations.

title: Loss Prevention Managers
description: Plan and direct policies, procedures, or systems to prevent the loss of assets.

title: Agents and Business Managers of Artists, Performers, and Athletes
description: Represent and promote artists, performers, and athletes in dealings with current or prospective employers.

title: Buyers and Purchasing Agents, Farm Products
description: Purchase farm products either for further processing or resale.

title: Wholesale and Retail Buyers, Except Farm Products
description: Buy merchandise or commodities for resale to consumers at the wholesale or retail level.

title: Purchasing Agents, Except Wholesale, Retail, and Farm Products
description: Purchase machinery, equipment, tools, parts, supplies, or services necessary for the operation of an establishment.

title: Claims Adjusters, Examiners, and Investigators
description: Review settled claims to determine that payments and settlements are made in accordance with company practices and procedures.

title: Insurance Appraisers, Auto Damage
description: Appraise automobile or other vehicle damage to determine repair costs for insurance claim settlement.

title: Compliance Officers
description: Examine, evaluate, and investigate eligibility for or conformity with laws and regulations.

title: Government Property Inspectors and Investigators
description: Investigate or inspect government property to ensure compliance with contract agreements and government regulations.

title: Coroners
description: Direct activities such as autopsies, pathological and toxicological analyses, and inquests relating to the investigation of deaths.

title: Regulatory Affairs Specialists
description: Coordinate and document internal regulatory processes, such as internal audits, inspections, license renewals, or registrations.

title: Customs Brokers
description: Prepare customs documentation and ensure that shipments meet all applicable laws to facilitate the import and export of goods.

title: Cost Estimators
description: Prepare cost estimates for product manufacturing, construction projects, or services to aid management in bidding on or determining price of product or service.

title: Human Resources Specialists
description: Perform activities in the human resource area. Includes employment specialists who screen, recruit, interview, and place workers.

title: Farm Labor Contractors
description: Recruit and hire seasonal or temporary agricultural laborers.

title: Labor Relations Specialists
description: Resolve disputes between workers and managers, negotiate collective bargaining agreements, or coordinate grievance procedures.

title: Logisticians
description: Analyze and coordinate the logistical functions of a firm or organization.

title: Logistics Engineers
description: Design or analyze operational solutions for projects such as transportation optimization, network modeling, process and methods analysis.

title: Logistics Analysts
description: Analyze product delivery or supply chain processes to identify or recommend changes.

title: Project Management Specialists
description: Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis.

title: Management Analysts
description: Conduct organizational studies and evaluations, design systems and procedures, conduct work simplification and measurement studies.

title: Meeting, Convention, and Event Planners
description: Coordinate activities of staff, convention personnel, or clients to make arrangements for group meetings, events, or conventions.

title: Fundraisers
description: Organize activities to raise funds or otherwise solicit and gather monetary donations or other gifts for an organization.

title: Compensation, Benefits, and Job Analysis Specialists
description: Conduct programs of compensation and benefits and job analysis for employer.

title: Training and Development Specialists
description: Design and conduct training and development programs to improve individual and organizational performance.

title: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
description: Research conditions in local, regional, national, or online markets. Gather information to determine potential sales of a product or service.

title: Search Marketing Strategists
description: Employ search marketing tactics to increase visibility and engagement with content, products, or services in Internet-enabled devices.

title: Business Continuity Planners
description: Develop, maintain, or implement business responses and continuity strategies to respond to emergencies and disruptions.

title: Online Merchants
description: Conduct retail activities of businesses operating exclusively online.

title: Security Management Specialists
description: Conduct security assessments for organizations, and design security systems and processes.

title: Accountants and Auditors
description: Examine, analyze, and interpret accounting records to prepare financial statements, give advice, or audit and evaluate statements prepared by others.

title: Appraisers of Personal and Business Property
description: Appraise personal or business property, such as art, antiques, jewelry, or machinery.

title: Appraisers and Assessors of Real Estate
description: Appraise real estate and estimate its fair value. May assess taxes in accordance with prescribed schedules.

title: Budget Analysts
description: Analyze proposed business plans and financial requests to prepare organizational budgets. They monitor institutional spending to ensure financial compliance and stability.

title: Credit Analysts
description: Evaluate the financial history and current economic status of individuals or businesses to determine creditworthiness. They assess risk and recommend loan approvals or credit limits.

title: Financial and Investment Analysts
description: Conduct quantitative analyses of information affecting investment programs of public or private institutions. They provide guidance to businesses and individuals making investment decisions.

title: Personal Financial Advisors
description: Provide advice on investments, insurance, mortgages, college savings, estate planning, and taxes to help individuals manage their finances. They assess clients' financial needs and goals.

title: Insurance Underwriters
description: Review insurance applications to evaluate the risk involved in insuring people or assets. They determine pricing and coverage amounts based on risk analysis.

title: Financial Risk Specialists
description: Identify and analyze areas of potential risk threatening the assets, earning capacity, or success of organizations. They develop strategies to mitigate financial exposure.

title: Financial Examiners
description: Ensure compliance with laws governing financial institutions and transactions. They review balance sheets, evaluate the risk level of loans, and assess bank management.

title: Credit Counselors
description: Advise and educate individuals or organizations on acquiring and managing debt. They may provide guidance in determining the best type of loan or developing a debt repayment plan.

title: Loan Officers
description: Evaluate, authorize, or recommend approval of commercial, real estate, or credit loans. They advise borrowers on financial status and methods of payments.

title: Tax Examiners and Collectors, and Revenue Agents
description: Determine tax liability or collect taxes from individuals or business firms according to prescribed laws and regulations. They review tax returns and conduct audits.

title: Tax Preparers
description: Prepare tax returns for individuals or small businesses. They compute taxes owed and ensure compliance with tax regulations.

title: Financial Quantitative Analysts
description: Develop quantitative financial products used to inform individuals or financial institutions engaged in saving, lending, investing, borrowing, or managing risk.

title: Fraud Examiners, Investigators and Analysts
description: Obtain evidence, take statements, produce reports, and testify to findings regarding resolution of fraud allegations. They analyze financial records to detect irregular activities.

title: Computer Systems Analysts
description: Study an organization's current computer systems and procedures, and design solutions to help the organization operate more efficiently and effectively.

title: Health Informatics Specialists
description: Apply knowledge of nursing and informatics to assist in the design, development, and ongoing modification of computerized health care systems.

title: Information Security Analysts
description: Plan, implement, upgrade, or monitor security measures for the protection of computer networks and information. They respond to computer security breaches and viruses.

title: Computer and Information Research Scientists
description: Invent and design new approaches to computing technology and find innovative uses for existing technology. They study and solve complex problems in computing for business, medicine, and science.

title: Computer Network Support Specialists
description: Analyze, test, troubleshoot, and evaluate existing network systems, such as local area networks (LAN), wide area networks (WAN), and Internet systems.

title: Computer User Support Specialists
description: Provide technical assistance to computer users. Answer questions or resolve computer problems for clients in person, via telephone, or electronically.

title: Computer Network Architects
description: Design and build data communication networks, including local area networks (LANs), wide area networks (WANs), and Intranets. They upgrade hardware and software components.

title: Telecommunications Engineering Specialists
description: Design or configure voice, video, and data communications systems. Supervise installation and post-installation service and maintenance.

title: Database Administrators
description: Administer, test, and implement computer databases, applying knowledge of database management systems. Coordinate changes to computer databases.

title: Database Architects
description: Design strategies for enterprise databases, data warehouse systems, and multidimensional networks. Set standards for database operations, programming, query processes, and security.

title: Data Warehousing Specialists
description: Design, model, or implement corporate data warehousing activities. Program and configure data warehouses and provide support to warehouse users.

title: Network and Computer Systems Administrators
description: Install, configure, and maintain an organization's local area network (LAN), wide area network (WAN), data communications network, operating systems, and physical and virtual servers.

title: Computer Programmers
description: Create, modify, and test the code and scripts that allow computer applications to run. Work from specifications drawn up by software and web developers.

title: Software Developers
description: Research, design, and develop computer and network software or specialized utility programs. Update software and analyze user needs and software requirements.

title: Software Quality Assurance Analysts and Testers
description: Develop and execute software test plans in order to identify software problems and their causes. Document software defects and report defects to software developers.

title: Web Developers
description: Design, create, and modify web sites. Analyze user needs to implement web site content, graphics, performance, and capacity.

title: Web and Digital Interface Designers
description: Design digital user interfaces or websites. Develop and test layouts, interfaces, functionality, and navigation menus to ensure compatibility and usability.

title: Video Game Designers
description: Design core features of video games. Specify innovative game and role-play mechanics, storylines, and character biographies.

title: Web Administrators
description: Manage web environment design, deploy, development, and maintenance activities. Perform testing and quality assurance of web sites and web applications.

title: Geographic Information Systems Technologists and Technicians
description: Assist scientists, technologists, or related professionals in building, maintaining, modifying, or using geographic information systems (GIS) databases.

title: Document Management Specialists
description: Implement and administer enterprise-wide document management systems and related procedures that allow organizations to capture, store, retrieve, share, and destroy electronic records.

title: Penetration Testers
description: Perform security assessments of computer systems, networks, and applications by simulating cyberattacks to identify vulnerabilities.

title: Information Security Engineers
description: Design, implement, and maintain security systems, protocols, and policies to protect an organization's network architecture from cyber threats.

title: Digital Forensics Analysts
description: Investigate cybercrimes by collecting, preserving, and analyzing digital evidence from computers, networks, and mobile devices.

title: Blockchain Engineers
description: Develop and implement digital blockchain for enterprise technology solutions. They design architecture, smart contracts, and decentralized applications.

title: Computer Systems Engineers/Architects
description: Design and develop solutions to complex applications problems, system administration issues, or network concerns. Perform systems management and integration functions.

title: Information Technology Project Managers
description: Plan, initiate, and manage information technology (IT) projects. Lead and guide the work of technical staff and serve as liaison between business and technical aspects of projects.

title: Actuaries
description: Analyze statistical data, such as mortality, accident, sickness, disability, and retirement rates and construct probability tables to forecast risk and liability for payment of future benefits.

title: Mathematicians
description: Conduct research in fundamental mathematics or in application of mathematical techniques to science, management, and other fields.

title: Operations Research Analysts
description: Formulate and apply mathematical modeling and other optimizing methods to develop and interpret information that assists management with decision making, policy formulation, or other managerial functions.

title: Statisticians
description: Develop or apply mathematical or statistical theory and methods to collect, organize, interpret, and summarize numerical data to provide usable information.

title: Biostatisticians
description: Develop and apply biostatistical theory and methods to the study of life sciences. Analyze health care, biological, and genomic data.

title: Data Scientists
description: Develop and implement a set of techniques or analytics applications to transform raw data into meaningful information using data-oriented programming languages and visualization software.

title: Business Intelligence Analysts
description: Produce financial and market intelligence by querying data repositories and generating periodic reports. Devise methods for identifying data patterns and trends in available information sources.

title: Clinical Data Managers
description: Apply knowledge of health care and database management to analyze clinical data, and to identify and report trends. Develop data management plans.

title: Bioinformatics Technicians
description: Apply bioinformatics principles to analyze, manage, and visualize genomic and biological data sets. Provide technical support to bioinformatics researchers.

title: Architects, Except Landscape and Naval
description: Plan and design structures, such as private residences, office buildings, theaters, factories, and other structural property.

title: Landscape Architects
description: Plan and design land areas for projects such as parks and other recreational facilities, airports, highways, hospitals, schools, land subdivisions, and commercial, industrial, and residential sites.

title: Cartographers and Photogrammetrists
description: Collect, analyze, and interpret geographic information provided by geodetic surveys, aerial photographs, and satellite data. Research, study, and prepare maps and other spatial data in digital or graphic form.

title: Surveyors
description: Make exact measurements and determine property boundaries. Provide data relevant to the shape, contour, gravitation, location, elevation, or dimension of land or land features on or near the earth's surface.

title: Geodetic Surveyors
description: Measure large areas of the Earth's surface using satellite observations, global navigation satellite systems (GNSS), light detection and ranging (LIDAR), or related sources.

title: Aerospace Engineers
description: Perform engineering duties in designing, constructing, and testing aircraft, missiles, and spacecraft. May conduct basic and applied research to evaluate adaptability of materials and equipment to aircraft design and manufacture.

title: Agricultural Engineers
description: Apply knowledge of engineering technology and biological science to agricultural problems concerned with power and machinery, electrification, structures, soil and water conservation, and processing of agricultural products.

title: Bioengineers and Biomedical Engineers
description: Apply knowledge of engineering, biology, and biomechanical principles to the design, development, and evaluation of biological and health systems and products, such as artificial organs, prostheses, instrumentation, medical information systems, and health management and care delivery systems.

title: Chemical Engineers
description: Design chemical plant equipment and devise processes for manufacturing chemicals and products, such as gasoline, synthetic rubber, plastics, detergents, cement, paper, and pulp.

title: Civil Engineers
description: Perform engineering duties in planning, designing, and overseeing construction and maintenance of building structures, and facilities, such as roads, railroads, airports, bridges, harbors, channels, dams, irrigation projects, pipelines, power plants, and water and sewage systems.

title: Transportation Engineers
description: Develop plans for surface transportation projects, according to established engineering standards and state or federal construction policy. Prepare designs, specifications, or estimates for transportation facilities.

title: Computer Hardware Engineers
description: Research, design, develop, or test computer or computer-related equipment for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use. May supervise the manufacturing and installation of computer or computer-related equipment and components.

title: Electrical Engineers
description: Research, design, develop, test, or supervise the manufacturing and installation of electrical equipment, components, or systems for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use.

title: Electronics Engineers, Except Computer
description: Research, design, develop, or test electronic components and systems for commercial, industrial, military, or scientific use employing knowledge of electronic theory and materials properties.

title: Radio Frequency Identification Device Specialists
description: Design and implement radio frequency identification device (RFID) systems used to track shipments or goods.

title: Environmental Engineers
description: Research, design, plan, or perform engineering duties in the prevention, control, and remediation of environmental hazards using various engineering disciplines.

title: Health and Safety Engineers, Except Mining Safety Engineers and Inspectors
description: Promote worksite or product safety by applying knowledge of industrial processes, mechanics, chemistry, psychology, and industrial health and safety law.

title: Fire-Prevention and Protection Engineers
description: Research causes of fires, determine fire protection methods, and design or recommend materials or equipment such as structural components or fire-detection equipment to assist organizations in safeguarding life and property against fire, explosion, and related hazards.

title: Industrial Engineers
description: Design, develop, test, and evaluate integrated systems for managing industrial production processes, including human work factors, quality control, inventory control, logistics and material flow, cost analysis, and production coordination.

title: Human Factors Engineers and Ergonomists
description: Design objects, facilities, and environments to optimize human well-being and overall system performance, applying theory, principles, and data regarding the relationship between humans and respective technology.

title: Validation Engineers
description: Design or plan protocols for equipment or processes to produce products meeting internal and external purity, safety, and quality requirements.

title: Manufacturing Engineers
description: Design, integrate, or improve manufacturing systems or related processes. May work with commercial or industrial designers to refine product designs to increase producibility and decrease costs.

title: Marine Engineers and Naval Architects
description: Design, develop, and evaluate the operation of marine vessels, ship machinery, and related equipment, such as power supply and propulsion systems.

title: Materials Engineers
description: Evaluate materials and develop machinery and processes to manufacture materials for use in products that must meet specialized design and performance specifications.

title: Mechanical Engineers
description: Perform engineering duties in planning and designing tools, engines, machines, and other mechanically functioning equipment. Oversee installation, operation, maintenance, and repair of such equipment.

title: Fuel Cell Engineers
description: Fuel Cell Engineers design, develop, and test fuel cell systems that convert chemical energy into electrical energy. They work on improving efficiency, durability, and cost-effectiveness of fuel cell technologies for applications in transportation, stationary power, and portable devices. These engineers collaborate with materials scientists and other engineers to advance clean energy solutions.

title: Automotive Engineers
description: Automotive Engineers research, design, and develop vehicles and their subsystems, including engines, transmissions, and electrical systems. They work to improve vehicle performance, safety, fuel efficiency, and manufacturability. These professionals collaborate across disciplines to bring new automotive technologies from concept to production.

title: Mining and Geological Engineers, Including Mining Safety Engineers
description: Mining and Geological Engineers design mines and plan the extraction of minerals, metals, and other geological resources. They ensure operations are efficient, economical, and compliant with safety and environmental regulations. Mining Safety Engineers specifically focus on identifying and mitigating hazards to protect workers.

title: Nuclear Engineers
description: Nuclear Engineers research and develop the processes, instruments, and systems used to derive benefits from nuclear energy and radiation. They design and operate nuclear power plants, and work on applications in medicine, industry, and defense. Ensuring reactor safety and managing radioactive materials are central responsibilities.

title: Petroleum Engineers
description: Petroleum Engineers design and develop methods for extracting oil and gas from deposits below the earth's surface. They evaluate reservoirs, plan drilling operations, and optimize production to maximize resource recovery while minimizing costs and environmental impact. They work closely with geoscientists and other specialists throughout the extraction lifecycle.

title: Energy Engineers, Except Wind and Solar
description: Energy Engineers design systems and solutions to improve energy production, distribution, and consumption efficiency across various non-renewable and non-wind/solar sectors. They analyze energy use in buildings, industrial processes, and infrastructure to identify optimization opportunities. Their work supports sustainability goals and regulatory compliance.

title: Mechatronics Engineers
description: Mechatronics Engineers integrate principles of mechanical, electrical, and computer engineering to design and develop automated and intelligent systems. They work on products such as robotics, smart appliances, and manufacturing equipment. Their interdisciplinary approach enables the creation of sophisticated systems that combine sensing, actuation, and control.

title: Microsystems Engineers
description: Microsystems Engineers design and develop miniaturized devices and systems, including microelectromechanical systems (MEMS), for applications in sensors, actuators, and biomedical devices. They apply principles from electrical, mechanical, and materials engineering at microscale levels. Their work enables innovations in consumer electronics, healthcare, and aerospace.

title: Photonics Engineers
description: Photonics Engineers research, design, and develop technologies that generate, detect, and manipulate light for applications in telecommunications, medical devices, and sensing systems. They work with lasers, fiber optics, and optical components to build advanced photonic systems. Their expertise bridges physics and engineering to solve complex light-based challenges.

title: Robotics Engineers
description: Robotics Engineers design, build, and test robotic systems for use in manufacturing, healthcare, defense, and other industries. They integrate mechanical, electrical, and software components to create robots capable of performing complex tasks autonomously or semi-autonomously. Continuous improvement of robot performance, safety, and reliability is a key focus.

title: Nanosystems Engineers
description: Nanosystems Engineers design and develop functional systems and devices at the nanometer scale for applications in electronics, medicine, and materials science. They apply interdisciplinary knowledge from physics, chemistry, and engineering to manipulate matter at atomic and molecular levels. Their innovations have transformative potential across multiple high-technology industries.

title: Architectural and Civil Drafters
description: Architectural and Civil Drafters prepare detailed technical drawings and plans for buildings, infrastructure, and land development projects. Using CAD software, they translate engineer and architect designs into precise schematics used in construction. Their accuracy and attention to detail are critical to ensuring projects are built to specification.

title: Electrical and Electronics Drafters
description: Electrical and Electronics Drafters create detailed wiring diagrams, circuit board layouts, and technical schematics for electrical and electronic systems. They work from engineer specifications to produce drawings used in manufacturing, construction, and maintenance. Proficiency in specialized CAD and EDA software is essential to this role.

title: Mechanical Drafters
description: Mechanical Drafters produce detailed technical drawings and plans for machinery, mechanical devices, and components. Using CAD tools, they document dimensions, tolerances, and material specifications needed for manufacturing and assembly. Their drawings are essential references throughout the product development and production lifecycle.

title: Aerospace Engineering and Operations Technologists and Technicians
description: Aerospace Engineering and Operations Technologists and Technicians assist engineers in the design, testing, and operation of aircraft, spacecraft, and related systems. They conduct tests, analyze data, and maintain technical documentation to support aerospace projects. Their hands-on technical support is critical to the safe and efficient development of aerospace technologies.

title: Civil Engineering Technologists and Technicians
description: Civil Engineering Technologists and Technicians assist civil engineers in planning, designing, and overseeing infrastructure projects such as roads, bridges, and utilities. They conduct site surveys, collect data, and prepare reports and drawings to support project execution. Their fieldwork and technical expertise bridge the gap between engineering design and construction.

title: Electrical and Electronic Engineering Technologists and Technicians
description: Electrical and Electronic Engineering Technologists and Technicians assist in the design, development, testing, and maintenance of electrical and electronic equipment and systems. They troubleshoot faults, assemble prototypes, and document technical findings to support engineering teams. Their practical skills are vital to the reliable operation of electrical infrastructure and devices.

title: Electro-Mechanical and Mechatronics Technologists and Technicians
description: Electro-Mechanical and Mechatronics Technologists and Technicians operate, test, and maintain automated equipment that integrates electrical, mechanical, and computer systems. They diagnose malfunctions and perform repairs to keep complex automated systems running efficiently. Their interdisciplinary skill set supports advanced manufacturing and industrial automation environments.

title: Robotics Technicians
description: Robotics Technicians install, maintain, and repair robotic systems used in manufacturing and other industries. They troubleshoot hardware and software issues to minimize downtime and ensure robotic systems operate safely and efficiently. Working closely with engineers and operators, they play a key role in optimizing automated production processes.

title: Environmental Engineering Technologists and Technicians
description: Environmental Engineering Technologists and Technicians assist in developing and implementing solutions to environmental problems such as pollution control and waste management. They collect environmental samples, conduct tests, and compile data to support environmental assessments and regulatory compliance. Their fieldwork and laboratory skills are essential to monitoring and protecting environmental quality.

title: Industrial Engineering Technologists and Technicians
description: Industrial Engineering Technologists and Technicians assist industrial engineers in improving efficiency and productivity within manufacturing and production processes. They study workflows, collect time-and-motion data, and help implement process improvements. Their analytical support enables organizations to reduce waste and optimize resource utilization.

title: Nanotechnology Engineering Technologists and Technicians
description: Nanotechnology Engineering Technologists and Technicians assist in the research, fabrication, and testing of nanoscale materials and devices. They operate specialized equipment in clean room environments and prepare samples for characterization and analysis. Their technical support accelerates development in fields such as electronics, medicine, and advanced materials.

title: Mechanical Engineering Technologists and Technicians
description: Mechanical Engineering Technologists and Technicians assist mechanical engineers in designing, testing, and maintaining mechanical systems and equipment. They build prototypes, conduct performance tests, and document results to support product development and quality assurance. Their practical expertise bridges engineering design with real-world manufacturing and testing.

title: Automotive Engineering Technicians
description: Automotive Engineering Technicians assist in the development, testing, and evaluation of vehicles and automotive components. They conduct diagnostic tests, gather performance data, and support engineers in validating design changes. Their hands-on technical skills are critical to ensuring vehicles meet safety, performance, and regulatory standards.

title: Calibration Technologists and Technicians
description: Calibration Technologists and Technicians test, adjust, and certify measurement instruments and equipment to ensure they meet established accuracy standards. They maintain calibration records, identify out-of-tolerance equipment, and perform corrective adjustments in laboratory and field settings. Their work is essential to quality assurance across manufacturing, aerospace, and scientific industries.

title: Non-Destructive Testing Specialists
description: Non-Destructive Testing Specialists inspect materials, components, and structures for flaws and defects without causing damage to the tested items. They use techniques such as ultrasonic, radiographic, magnetic particle, and dye penetrant testing to ensure structural integrity. Their findings are critical to safety and quality assurance in industries such as aerospace, construction, and manufacturing.

title: Photonics Technicians
description: Photonics Technicians assist in the assembly, testing, and maintenance of photonic devices such as lasers, fiber optic systems, and optical sensors. They set up and operate specialized optical test equipment and document performance results for engineering teams. Their precision and technical knowledge support advancements in telecommunications, healthcare, and defense systems.

title: Surveying and Mapping Technicians
description: Surveying and Mapping Technicians assist licensed surveyors in measuring and recording the physical features of land, air, and water areas. They operate surveying instruments, collect field data, and assist in the preparation of maps and legal property descriptions. Their accurate fieldwork is foundational to construction, land development, and geographic information projects.

title: Animal Scientists
description: Animal Scientists study the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, and development of domestic and farm animals. They conduct research to improve animal breeding programs, feed efficiency, and overall livestock health and productivity. Their findings support agricultural industries and contribute to advances in food safety and sustainability.

title: Food Scientists and Technologists
description: Food Scientists and Technologists research the physical, chemical, and biological nature of food to develop safe, nutritious, and appealing food products. They work on improving food processing techniques, extending shelf life, and ensuring compliance with food safety regulations. Their expertise spans product development, quality assurance, and regulatory affairs.

title: Soil and Plant Scientists
description: Soil and Plant Scientists study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils and their relationship to plant growth and crop production. They conduct field and laboratory research to develop sustainable agricultural practices and improve soil health. Their work informs land management decisions and supports food security initiatives.

title: Biochemists and Biophysicists
description: Biochemists and Biophysicists study the chemical and physical principles of living things and biological processes. They conduct research on topics such as cell development, heredity, and disease mechanisms, often with applications in medicine and pharmaceuticals. Their laboratory and analytical work is foundational to advances in biotechnology and drug development.

title: Microbiologists
description: Microbiologists study microorganisms such as bacteria, viruses, algae, fungi, and some parasites to understand their characteristics, growth, and interaction with their environments. They conduct laboratory research with applications in medicine, food safety, environmental science, and biotechnology. Their discoveries underpin developments in vaccines, antibiotics, and industrial bioprocesses.

title: Zoologists and Wildlife Biologists
description: Zoologists and Wildlife Biologists study the origins, behavior, diseases, genetics, and life processes of animals and wildlife. They conduct field and laboratory research to monitor animal populations and assess the impact of human activity on wildlife and ecosystems. Their findings inform conservation policies and wildlife management strategies.

title: Bioinformatics Scientists
description: Bioinformatics Scientists develop and apply computational tools and methods to analyze large biological datasets, particularly genomic and proteomic data. They bridge biology and data science to uncover insights into biological systems, disease mechanisms, and evolutionary relationships. Their work supports precision medicine, drug discovery, and genomic research.

title: Molecular and Cellular Biologists
description: Molecular and Cellular Biologists study the structure, function, and behavior of cells and molecules to understand fundamental biological processes. They conduct experiments involving gene expression, protein function, and cell signaling, often with implications for disease research and therapeutics. Their laboratory expertise is essential to advances in biotechnology and biomedical science.

title: Geneticists
description: Geneticists study genes, heredity, and genetic variation in living organisms to understand how traits are passed from one generation to the next. They conduct research with applications in medicine, agriculture, and evolutionary biology, including identifying genetic causes of disease. Their expertise supports genetic counseling, crop improvement, and personalized medicine.

title: Biologists
description: Biologists study living organisms and their relationship to the environment across a broad range of specializations. They design and conduct experiments, collect and analyze data, and publish findings to advance scientific knowledge. Their research contributes to fields including medicine, conservation, biotechnology, and environmental science.

title: Conservation Scientists
description: Conservation Scientists manage the overall quality and use of natural resources such as forests, rangelands, and soil to ensure their long-term sustainability. They work with landowners, government agencies, and communities to develop and implement land use and conservation plans. Their expertise helps balance ecological preservation with economic and recreational needs.

title: Range Managers
description: Range Managers study, manage, and improve rangeland resources to maximize their use for grazing livestock, wildlife, and watershed protection. They develop management plans that balance ecological sustainability with agricultural productivity and recreation. Their fieldwork includes monitoring vegetation, soil, and water conditions across large land areas.

title: Park Naturalists
description: Park Naturalists develop and present programs to inform the public about the natural, historical, and cultural features of parks and protected areas. They conduct environmental education activities, lead interpretive tours, and help visitors understand and appreciate natural ecosystems. Their work fosters environmental stewardship and public appreciation of conservation efforts.

title: Foresters
description: Foresters manage forested land for economic, recreational, and conservation purposes by developing and implementing forest management plans. They assess timber resources, oversee harvesting operations, and work to prevent wildfires, pest infestations, and environmental degradation. Their expertise supports sustainable forestry practices and the long-term health of forest ecosystems.

title: Epidemiologists
description: Epidemiologists investigate the causes, distribution, and control of diseases and health conditions in populations. They design and conduct studies, analyze health data, and develop recommendations to prevent and control public health threats. Their findings guide public health policy, outbreak response, and preventive health programs.

title: Medical Scientists, Except Epidemiologists
description: Medical Scientists conduct research aimed at improving overall human health by investigating the causes of diseases and developing new treatments or preventive measures. They design and execute clinical and laboratory studies, analyze results, and publish findings in peer-reviewed literature. Their work spans fields such as pharmacology, immunology, and pathology.

title: Astronomers
description: Astronomers observe, research, and interpret astronomical phenomena to increase knowledge of the universe, including stars, galaxies, black holes, and cosmological processes. They use ground-based and space-based telescopes along with advanced computational tools to collect and analyze data. Their research contributes to fundamental physics and our understanding of the origin and evolution of the universe.

title: Physicists
description: Physicists explore the fundamental laws governing matter, energy, space, and time through theoretical and experimental research. They develop and test theories, design experiments, and apply physical principles to solve problems across a wide range of scientific and technological fields. Their work has broad implications for technology development, engineering, and our understanding of the natural world.

title: Atmospheric and Space Scientists
description: Atmospheric and Space Scientists study the physical characteristics, motions, and processes of the Earth's atmosphere and near-space environment. They develop weather forecasts, study climate change, and investigate space weather phenomena that affect satellite and communication systems. Their research supports aviation safety, disaster preparedness, and environmental policy.

title: Chemists
description: Chemists study the composition, structure, properties, and reactions of matter at the atomic and molecular level. They conduct laboratory research and develop new substances, materials, and processes with applications in pharmaceuticals, manufacturing, and environmental science. Their analytical expertise supports quality control, product development, and scientific discovery.

title: Materials Scientists
description: Materials Scientists research and study the structures and chemical properties of natural and synthetic materials to develop new or improved products and processes. They investigate materials such as metals, ceramics, polymers, and composites to understand their properties at the atomic and molecular level. Their discoveries enable innovations in electronics, aerospace, and biomedical engineering.

title: Environmental Scientists and Specialists, Including Health
description: Environmental Scientists and Specialists use their knowledge of natural sciences to protect the environment and human health by identifying and addressing environmental problems. They collect and analyze environmental samples, assess pollution sources, and develop remediation strategies in compliance with regulations. Their work spans consulting, government, and research sectors.

title: Geoscientists, Except Hydrologists and Geographers
description: Geoscientists study the composition, structure, processes, and history of the Earth through fieldwork, laboratory analysis, and computational modeling. They apply their knowledge to locate natural resources, assess natural hazards, and address environmental challenges. Their expertise supports industries including mining, oil and gas, environmental consulting, and government agencies.

title: Hydrologists
description: Hydrologists study the distribution, circulation, and physical properties of surface and underground waters. They analyze data on precipitation, groundwater levels, and river flows to address issues related to water supply, flood control, and environmental management. Their research informs water resource policy and infrastructure planning.

title: Remote Sensing Scientists and Technologists
description: Remote Sensing Scientists and Technologists analyze data collected by satellite and airborne sensors to study the Earth's surface, atmosphere, and environment. They process and interpret imagery for applications including land use mapping, disaster monitoring, and climate research. Their expertise combines earth science knowledge with advanced image processing and GIS skills.

title: Economists
description: Economists study the production, distribution, and consumption of goods and services by analyzing economic data and developing models to explain trends and forecast outcomes. They advise governments, businesses, and organizations on economic policy, resource allocation, and market conditions. Their quantitative and analytical expertise informs decision-making across public and private sectors.

title: Survey Researchers
description: Survey Researchers design and conduct surveys and analyze data to collect information about people's opinions, knowledge, and behaviors. They develop questionnaires, select sampling methodologies, and apply statistical techniques to interpret results for academic, governmental, and commercial purposes. Their findings support market research, policy development, and public opinion analysis.

title: Industrial-Organizational Psychologists
description: Industrial-Organizational Psychologists apply psychological principles and research methods to workplace settings to improve productivity, employee well-being, and organizational effectiveness. They study topics such as personnel selection, performance evaluation, training, and organizational culture. Their expertise is used by corporations, government agencies, and consulting firms to optimize human resources.

title: Clinical and Counseling Psychologists
description: Clinical and Counseling Psychologists assess, diagnose, and treat individuals experiencing mental health conditions, emotional disturbances, and behavioral problems. They use evidence-based therapeutic approaches such as cognitive-behavioral therapy to support clients across diverse settings including hospitals, clinics, and private practice. Their work promotes mental health and psychological well-being.

title: School Psychologists
description: School Psychologists support the mental health, learning, and behavioral needs of students within educational settings. They conduct psychoeducational assessments, collaborate with teachers and parents, and design intervention plans to address academic and social-emotional challenges. Their work helps create supportive school environments that promote student success.

title: Neuropsychologists
description: Neuropsychologists evaluate and treat individuals with brain injuries, neurological conditions, and cognitive impairments by examining the relationship between brain function and behavior. They administer comprehensive neuropsychological assessments to diagnose conditions and guide rehabilitation and treatment planning. Their expertise bridges clinical psychology and neuroscience.

title: Clinical Neuropsychologists
description: Clinical Neuropsychologists specialize in assessing and treating cognitive, behavioral, and emotional disorders resulting from neurological conditions such as traumatic brain injury, dementia, and stroke. They conduct detailed evaluations using standardized tests to inform diagnosis, treatment, and legal or educational decisions. Their work is integral to neurology departments, rehabilitation centers, and forensic settings.

title: Sociologists
description: Sociologists study society, social institutions, and social relationships to understand how human behavior is shaped by group dynamics, culture, and social structures. They conduct surveys, interviews, and statistical analyses to research topics such as inequality, social mobility, and community behavior. Their findings inform social policy, organizational strategies, and public programs.

title: Urban and Regional Planners
description: Urban and Regional Planners develop land use plans and programs that help create communities, accommodate population growth, and revitalize physical facilities. They analyze demographic data, assess infrastructure needs, and work with government officials and stakeholders to design sustainable communities. Their planning decisions shape transportation networks, housing, and public spaces.

title: Anthropologists and Archeologists
description: Anthropologists and Archeologists study the origin, development, and behavior of human beings and their cultures through fieldwork, artifact analysis, and historical records. They excavate sites, analyze cultural remains, and produce research that illuminates human history, social organization, and cultural practices. Their findings contribute to museum collections, heritage conservation, and academic scholarship.

title: Geographers
description: Geographers study the physical and human characteristics of the Earth's surface, including the spatial distribution of populations, resources, and environmental phenomena. They use GIS, remote sensing, and field research to analyze geographic patterns and inform decisions in urban planning, environmental management, and policy. Their work bridges the natural and social sciences.

title: Historians
description: Historians research, analyze, and interpret past events and their significance by examining primary sources such as documents, artifacts, and oral histories. They write scholarly works, teach, and provide historical context for public policy, legal cases, and cultural institutions. Their expertise preserves cultural heritage and promotes informed understanding of contemporary issues.

title: Political Scientists
description: Political Scientists study political systems, government structures, public policies, and international relations to understand how power is distributed and exercised. They conduct research using quantitative and qualitative methods, publish findings, and advise governments, think tanks, and corporations. Their analysis contributes to policy development, electoral strategy, and public discourse.

title: Transportation Planners
description: Transportation Planners develop strategies and policies for transportation systems including roads, public transit, cycling infrastructure, and freight networks. They collect and analyze traffic and demographic data to forecast transportation needs and design solutions that improve mobility and accessibility. Their work supports sustainable urban development and regional connectivity.

title: Agricultural Technicians
description: Agricultural Technicians work alongside agricultural scientists to conduct experiments and collect data aimed at improving crop yields, animal health, and farming practices. They maintain laboratory and field equipment, prepare samples, and record experimental results under scientific supervision. Their hands-on support accelerates agricultural research and development.

title: Precision Agriculture Technicians
description: Precision Agriculture Technicians apply technology such as GPS, drones, and remote sensing tools to optimize farming practices and improve crop management. They collect and analyze field data to support site-specific management decisions that maximize efficiency and reduce resource waste. Their technical expertise bridges agricultural science and modern data-driven farming.

title: Food Science Technicians
description: Food Science Technicians assist food scientists in researching, developing, and testing food products and processing methods to ensure safety, quality, and regulatory compliance. They conduct laboratory tests, prepare samples, and maintain detailed records of experimental procedures and results. Their technical support is critical to product development and quality assurance in the food industry.

title: Biological Technicians
description: Biological Technicians assist biological and medical scientists in conducting laboratory research and experiments. They set up and operate scientific equipment, prepare specimens, and collect and analyze biological data under the direction of senior researchers. Their meticulous technical work supports discoveries in medicine, agriculture, and environmental science.

title: Chemical Technicians
description: Chemical Technicians assist chemists and chemical engineers in developing, testing, and producing chemical products and processes. They operate laboratory instruments, conduct routine analyses, and maintain detailed experimental records in industrial, research, and quality control settings. Their technical skills support the safe and efficient operation of chemical laboratories and production facilities.

title: Environmental Science and Protection Technicians, Including Health
description: Environmental Science and Protection Technicians monitor the environment and investigate sources of pollution and contamination to protect public health and the environment. They collect air, water, and soil samples, perform laboratory tests, and compile data for regulatory reporting and remediation efforts. Their fieldwork and analytical skills support compliance with environmental laws and standards.

title: Geological Technicians, Except Hydrologic Technicians
description: Geological Technicians assist geoscientists in exploring and extracting natural resources by collecting and analyzing geological samples and data. They operate surveying and testing equipment, prepare geological maps, and document field and laboratory findings. Their technical support is essential to mining, oil and gas exploration, and environmental assessment projects.

title: Hydrologic Technicians
description: Hydrologic Technicians assist hydrologists in collecting, measuring, and analyzing data related to surface water and groundwater resources. They install and maintain monitoring equipment, conduct field measurements, and prepare data reports to support water resource management and environmental studies. Their fieldwork provides critical data for flood forecasting, water quality assessment, and conservation planning.

title: Nuclear Technicians
description: Assist physicists, engineers, and other professionals in nuclear research and energy production.

title: Nuclear Monitoring Technicians
description: Collect and test samples to monitor results of nuclear experiments and contamination.

title: Social Science Research Assistants
description: Assist social scientists in laboratory, survey, and other social science research.

title: Forest and Conservation Technicians
description: Provide technical assistance regarding the conservation of soil, water, forests, or related natural resources.

title: Forensic Science Technicians
description: Collect, identify, classify, and analyze physical evidence related to criminal investigations.

title: Quality Control Analysts
description: Conduct tests to determine the quality of raw materials, bulk intermediate, and finished products.

title: Remote Sensing Technicians
description: Apply remote sensing principles and methods to analyze data and solve problems in natural resource management.

title: Occupational Health and Safety Specialists
description: Review and evaluate work environments to design programs and procedures that control, eliminate, and prevent disease or injury.

title: Occupational Health and Safety Technicians
description: Collect data on work environments for analysis by occupational health and safety specialists.

title: Substance Abuse and Behavioral Disorder Counselors
description: Counsel and advise individuals with alcohol, tobacco, drug, or other behavioral problems.

title: Educational, Guidance, and Career Counselors and Advisors
description: Advise and assist students and provide educational and vocational guidance services.

title: Marriage and Family Therapists
description: Diagnose and treat mental and emotional disorders within the context of marriage and family systems.

title: Mental Health Counselors
description: Counsel individuals and groups to promote optimum mental and emotional health, with an emphasis on prevention.

title: Rehabilitation Counselors
description: Counsel individuals to maximize the independence and employability of persons coping with personal, social, and vocational difficulties.

title: Child, Family, and School Social Workers
description: Provide social services and assistance to improve the social and psychological functioning of children and their families.

title: Healthcare Social Workers
description: Provide individuals, families, and groups with the psychosocial support needed to cope with chronic, acute, or terminal illnesses.

title: Mental Health and Substance Abuse Social Workers
description: Assess and treat individuals with mental, emotional, or substance abuse problems, including abuse of alcohol, tobacco, and/or other drugs.

title: Health Education Specialists
description: Provide and manage health education programs that help individuals, families, and their communities maximize and maintain healthy lifestyles.

title: Probation Officers and Correctional Treatment Specialists
description: Provide social services to assist in rehabilitation of law offenders in custody or on probation or parole.

title: Social and Human Service Assistants
description: Assist in providing client services in a wide variety of fields, such as psychology, rehabilitation, or social work.

title: Community Health Workers
description: Assist individuals and communities to adopt healthy behaviors and conduct outreach for medical personnel or health organizations.

title: Clergy
description: Conduct religious worship and perform other spiritual functions associated with beliefs and practices of religious faith or denomination.

title: Directors, Religious Activities and Education
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate denominational or congregational programs of religious education or activities.

title: Lawyers
description: Represent clients in criminal and civil litigation and other legal proceedings, draw up legal documents, or manage or advise clients on legal transactions.

title: Judicial Law Clerks
description: Assist judges in court or by conducting research or preparing legal documents.

title: Administrative Law Judges, Adjudicators, and Hearing Officers
description: Conduct hearings to recommend or make decisions on claims concerning government programs or other government-related matters.

title: Arbitrators, Mediators, and Conciliators
description: Facilitate negotiation and conflict resolution through dialogue to resolve conflicts outside of the court system.

title: Judges, Magistrate Judges, and Magistrates
description: Arbitrate, advise, adjudicate, or administer justice in a court of law.

title: Paralegals and Legal Assistants
description: Assist lawyers by investigating facts, preparing legal documents, or researching legal precedent.

title: Title Examiners, Abstractors, and Searchers
description: Search real estate records, examine titles, or summarize pertinent legal or insurance documents or details for a variety of purposes.

title: Business Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in business administration and management, such as accounting, finance, human resources, labor and industrial relations, marketing, and operations research.

title: Computer Science Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in computer science. May specialize in a field of computer science, such as the design and function of computers or operations and research analysis.

title: Mathematical Science Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses pertaining to mathematical concepts, statistics, and actuarial science and to the application of original and standardized mathematical techniques in solving specific problems and situations.

title: Architecture Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in architecture and architectural design, such as architectural environmental design, interior architecture/design, and landscape architecture.

title: Engineering Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses pertaining to the application of physical laws and principles of engineering for the development of machines, materials, instruments, processes, and services.

title: Agricultural Sciences Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in the agricultural sciences. Includes teachers of agronomy, dairy sciences, fisheries management, horticultural sciences, poultry sciences, range management, and agricultural soil conservation.

title: Biological Science Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in biological sciences.

title: Forestry and Conservation Science Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in environmental and conservation science.

title: Atmospheric, Earth, Marine, and Space Sciences Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in the physical sciences, except chemistry and physics. Includes teachers of subjects such as astronomy, climatology, paleontology, oceanography, and meteorology.

title: Chemistry Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses pertaining to the chemical and physical properties and compositional changes of substances.

title: Environmental Science Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in environmental science.

title: Physics Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses pertaining to the laws of matter and energy.

title: Anthropology and Archeology Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in anthropology or archeology.

title: Area, Ethnic, and Cultural Studies Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses pertaining to the culture and development of an area, an ethnic group, or any other group.

title: Economics Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in economics.

title: Geography Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in geography.

title: Political Science Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in political science, international affairs, and international relations.

title: Psychology Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in psychology, such as child, clinical, and developmental psychology, and psychological counseling.

title: Sociology Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in sociology.

title: Health Specialties Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in health specialties, in fields such as dentistry, laboratory technology, medicine, pharmacy, public health, therapy, and veterinary medicine.

title: Nursing Instructors and Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Demonstrate and teach patient care in classroom and clinical units to nursing students.

title: Education Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses pertaining to education, such as counseling, curriculum, guidance, instruction, teacher education, and teaching English as a second language.

title: Library Science Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in library science.

title: Criminal Justice and Law Enforcement Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in criminal justice, corrections, and law enforcement administration.

title: Law Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in law.

title: Social Work Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in social work.

title: Art, Drama, and Music Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in drama, music, and the arts including fine and applied art, such as painting and sculpture, or design and crafts.

title: Communications Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in communications, such as organizational communications, public relations, radio/television broadcasting, and journalism.

title: English Language and Literature Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in English language and literature, including linguistics and comparative literature.

title: Foreign Language and Literature Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach languages and literature courses in languages other than English.

title: History Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in human history and historiography.

title: Philosophy and Religion Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in philosophy, religion, and theology.

title: Family and Consumer Sciences Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses in childcare, family relations, finance, nutrition, and related subjects pertaining to home management.

title: Recreation and Fitness Studies Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach courses pertaining to recreation, leisure, and fitness studies, including exercise physiology and facilities management.

title: Career/Technical Education Teachers, Postsecondary
description: Teach occupational, vocational, career, or technical subjects to students at the postsecondary level.

title: Preschool Teachers, Except Special Education
description: Instruct preschool-aged children in activities designed to promote social, physical, and intellectual growth needed for primary school.

title: Kindergarten Teachers, Except Special Education
description: Teach elemental natural and social science, personal hygiene, music, art, and literature to kindergarten students.

title: Elementary School Teachers, Except Special Education
description: Teach academic and social skills to students at the elementary school level.

title: Middle School Teachers, Except Special and Career/Technical Education
description: Teach one or more subjects to students at the middle, intermediate, or junior high school level.

title: Career/Technical Education Teachers, Middle School
description: Teach occupational, vocational, career, or technical subjects to students at the middle school level.

title: Secondary School Teachers, Except Special and Career/Technical Education
description: Teach one or more subjects to students at the secondary school level.

title: Career/Technical Education Teachers, Secondary School
description: Teach occupational, vocational, career, or technical subjects to students at the secondary school level.

title: Special Education Teachers, Preschool
description: Teach preschool school subjects to educationally and physically handicapped students.

title: Special Education Teachers, Kindergarten
description: Teach kindergarten school subjects to educationally and physically handicapped students.

title: Special Education Teachers, Elementary School
description: Teach elementary school subjects to educationally and physically handicapped students.

title: Special Education Teachers, Middle School
description: Teach middle school subjects to educationally and physically handicapped students.

title: Special Education Teachers, Secondary School
description: Teach secondary school subjects to educationally and physically handicapped students.

title: Adapted Physical Education Specialists
description: Provide individualized physical education instruction or services to children, youth, or adults with exceptional physical needs.

title: Adult Basic Education, Adult Secondary Education, and English as a Second Language Instructors
description: Teach or instruct out-of-school youths and adults in remedial education classes, preparatory classes for the General Educational Development test, literacy, or English as a Second Language.

title: Self-Enrichment Teachers
description: Self-Enrichment Teachers instruct students in a variety of non-vocational subjects for personal interest and development, such as arts, cooking, fitness, and languages. They design and deliver engaging lessons tailored to adult or youth learners seeking to develop new hobbies and skills outside of formal academic settings. Their teaching fosters lifelong learning and personal growth.

title: Substitute Teachers, Short-Term
description: Substitute Teachers provide short-term classroom instruction in the absence of regular teachers across a variety of subjects and grade levels. They follow lesson plans left by the regular teacher, maintain classroom order, and ensure continuity of student learning. Adaptability and classroom management skills are essential to success in this role.

title: Tutors
description: Tutors provide individualized academic instruction and support to students who need additional help mastering specific subjects or skills. They assess student needs, develop personalized learning strategies, and monitor progress to improve academic performance. Tutors work in schools, private settings, or online environments across all education levels.

title: Archivists
description: Archivists appraise, process, catalog, and preserve permanent records and historically valuable documents for organizations, governments, and cultural institutions. They develop systems for organizing and providing access to archival materials in physical and digital formats. Their expertise ensures the long-term preservation of historical records and collective memory.

title: Curators
description: Curators oversee and manage collections of artwork, artifacts, or specimens in museums, galleries, and cultural institutions. They plan and develop exhibitions, conduct research, and work to acquire new items that align with their institution's mission and audience. Their expertise in collection management and public education shapes how cultural heritage is preserved and interpreted.

title: Museum Technicians and Conservators
description: Museum Technicians and Conservators prepare, restore, and maintain objects and specimens in museum collections to prevent deterioration and preserve their condition. They use scientific methods and specialized materials to clean, stabilize, and repair artifacts while documenting all conservation treatments. Their meticulous work ensures the long-term integrity of irreplaceable cultural and natural heritage.

title: Librarians and Media Collections Specialists
description: Librarians and Media Collections Specialists manage library collections, assist patrons in locating information, and develop programs to promote literacy and lifelong learning. They catalog and organize physical and digital resources, provide reference services, and curate collections to meet the needs of their communities. Their role has evolved to include digital literacy instruction and management of online databases.

title: Library Technicians
description: Library Technicians assist librarians in organizing and managing library collections and providing patron services. They check materials in and out, catalog new acquisitions, maintain shelving systems, and help patrons locate resources. Their support role is essential to the efficient day-to-day operation of public, academic, and special libraries.

title: Farm and Home Management Educators
description: Farm and Home Management Educators instruct farmers, families, and community members in agricultural practices, home economics, and related life skills. They develop and deliver educational programs on topics such as crop management, financial planning, nutrition, and sustainable living. Their outreach work, often through cooperative extension services, bridges research and practical community application.

title: Instructional Coordinators
description: Instructional Coordinators develop and oversee school curricula, teaching standards, and instructional materials to improve the quality of education. They evaluate teaching effectiveness, train teachers in new instructional methods, and align curricula with academic standards and assessment frameworks. Their leadership supports continuous improvement in educational outcomes across schools and districts.

title: Teaching Assistants, Preschool, Elementary, Middle, and Secondary School, Except Special Education
description: Teaching Assistants at the preschool through secondary level support lead teachers by working with individual students or small groups to reinforce learning and provide additional academic assistance. They help prepare instructional materials, monitor student behavior, and assist with classroom activities under teacher supervision. Their support allows teachers to focus on instruction while ensuring all students receive needed attention.

title: Teaching Assistants, Special Education
description: Special Education Teaching Assistants support students with disabilities by providing individualized assistance under the direction of special education teachers. They help implement Individualized Education Programs, assist with adaptive equipment, and support students' academic, social, and behavioral development in inclusive and specialized settings. Their compassionate, patient approach is critical to student progress and well-being.

title: Teaching Assistants, Postsecondary
