description: Postsecondary Teaching Assistants support college and university faculty by leading discussion sections, grading assignments, tutoring students, and assisting with course administration. They often pursue graduate degrees while gaining teaching experience and contributing to undergraduate instruction. Their role bridges faculty instruction and direct student support in higher education.

title: Art Directors
description: Art Directors are responsible for the visual style and imagery of publications, advertisements, film sets, and other media productions. They supervise teams of designers and artists, manage visual branding guidelines, and collaborate with clients and creative teams to ensure a cohesive aesthetic vision. Their leadership translates strategic communication goals into compelling visual outputs.

title: Craft Artists
description: Craft Artists create handmade objects for sale and exhibition using materials such as glass, textiles, wood, ceramics, and metal. They apply artistic techniques and creative vision to produce functional and decorative items with aesthetic and cultural value. Many craft artists market their work independently through galleries, craft fairs, and online platforms.

title: Fine Artists, Including Painters, Sculptors, and Illustrators
description: Fine Artists use a variety of media and techniques to create original artwork for aesthetic, cultural, and expressive purposes. Painters, sculptors, and illustrators develop personal styles and concepts, produce original works, and exhibit or sell their creations through galleries, commissions, and publications. Their work reflects artistic vision and contributes to cultural heritage.

title: Special Effects Artists and Animators
description: Special Effects Artists and Animators create visual effects, animated sequences, and digital imagery for film, television, video games, and other media. They use specialized software to design and produce motion graphics, character animations, and environmental effects that enhance storytelling and audience engagement. Their technical artistry combines creative vision with advanced digital production skills.

title: Commercial and Industrial Designers
description: Commercial and Industrial Designers develop the look, function, and usability of manufactured products ranging from consumer electronics to furniture and vehicles. They research user needs, sketch concepts, create prototypes, and collaborate with engineers and marketers to bring innovative products to market. Their work balances aesthetic appeal with practicality, safety, and manufacturability.

title: Fashion Designers
description: Fashion Designers create original clothing, accessories, and footwear by researching trends, sketching designs, selecting fabrics, and overseeing production. They work across haute couture, ready-to-wear, and mass-market sectors, developing collections that reflect both aesthetic vision and commercial viability. Their designs influence cultural expression and consumer identity.

title: Floral Designers
description: Floral Designers create decorative arrangements of flowers and plants for events, retail display, and personal gifting. They combine artistic creativity with knowledge of flowers, foliage, and design principles to produce visually appealing arrangements that meet client needs and budgets. Their work supports weddings, funerals, corporate events, and everyday retail sales.

title: Graphic Designers
description: Graphic Designers create visual content to communicate messages and ideas through typography, imagery, and layout for print and digital media. They work on projects such as branding, advertising, packaging, and web design, collaborating with clients and marketing teams to produce visually effective materials. Their design skills are fundamental to brand identity and effective communication.

title: Interior Designers
description: Interior Designers plan, design, and furnish interior spaces in residential, commercial, and institutional buildings to make them functional, safe, and aesthetically pleasing. They consult with clients, develop design concepts, select materials and furnishings, and coordinate with contractors and architects throughout the project. Their designs balance client vision with building codes, accessibility, and sustainability requirements.

title: Merchandise Displayers and Window Trimmers
description: Merchandise Displayers and Window Trimmers plan and create visual displays for retail stores, trade shows, and exhibitions to attract customers and promote products. They arrange merchandise, design window installations, and build themed displays that reflect brand identity and drive consumer engagement. Their visual merchandising skills directly influence retail sales performance.

title: Set and Exhibit Designers
description: Set and Exhibit Designers create the physical environments for theatrical productions, film and television sets, trade show exhibits, and museum installations. They develop design concepts, produce scaled drawings and models, and oversee construction to bring creative visions to life within budget and schedule constraints. Their spatial and narrative design skills shape audience experience and storytelling.

title: Actors
description: Actors portray characters in theatrical, film, television, and other dramatic productions by interpreting scripts and using voice, movement, and emotion to bring roles to life. They rehearse extensively, collaborate with directors and fellow cast members, and adapt performances across various media and formats. Building a professional career requires persistence, versatility, and continuous skill development.

title: Producers and Directors
description: Producers and Directors oversee the creative and logistical aspects of film, television, theater, and other media productions. Producers manage budgets, schedules, and personnel to bring projects to fruition, while Directors guide the artistic vision and performance quality of productions. Their leadership is critical to translating scripts and concepts into finished entertainment products.

title: Media Programming Directors
description: Media Programming Directors plan and oversee the selection and scheduling of content for broadcast television, radio, and digital media platforms. They analyze audience data and market trends to develop programming strategies that maximize viewership and meet organizational goals. Their decisions shape the editorial identity of media outlets and influence public consumption of content.

title: Talent Directors
description: Talent Directors identify, recruit, and manage performers and creative talent for productions in film, television, theater, and live events. They organize auditions, evaluate performers, and collaborate with producers and directors to cast appropriate talent for specific roles. Their industry relationships and eye for talent are essential to assembling high-caliber casts and creative teams.

title: Media Technical Directors/Managers
description: Media Technical Directors and Managers oversee the technical operations of broadcast studios, live productions, and media facilities. They coordinate camera operators, audio engineers, and other technical staff during live and recorded productions to ensure smooth execution. Their expertise ensures that technical elements align seamlessly with creative direction.

title: Athletes and Sports Competitors
description: Athletes and Sports Competitors participate in organized competitive sporting events at amateur or professional levels across a wide range of sports. They train rigorously to develop physical fitness, technical skills, and competitive strategies to perform at peak levels during competition. Their careers require dedication, physical conditioning, and often the support of coaches and sports science professionals.

title: Coaches and Scouts
description: Coaches instruct athletes in the fundamentals and strategies of their sport, develop training programs, and lead teams in practice and competition. Scouts evaluate and identify athletic talent for recruitment by teams, schools, and professional organizations. Both roles require deep sport-specific knowledge and the ability to assess and develop athletic potential.

title: Umpires, Referees, and Other Sports Officials
description: Umpires, Referees, and Sports Officials enforce the rules of athletic competitions, ensuring fair play and maintaining order during games and events. They observe action, make rule-based decisions, and resolve disputes to uphold the integrity of competition. Their impartiality and thorough knowledge of sport regulations are essential to the credibility of organized sports.

title: Dancers
description: Dancers perform choreographed routines in various styles including ballet, contemporary, hip-hop, and cultural dance forms for live audiences in theaters, on television, or in film. They train extensively to develop technical precision, artistry, and physical stamina, and often audition and collaborate with choreographers and directors. Their performances communicate emotion and storytelling through movement.

title: Choreographers
description: Choreographers create and arrange original dance sequences and movement patterns for performances in theater, film, television, and live events. They work closely with dancers, directors, and musicians to develop routines that serve the artistic and narrative goals of productions. Their creative vision and technical expertise in movement form the foundation of dance-based performances.

title: Music Directors and Composers
description: Music Directors lead orchestras, choirs, and other musical ensembles during rehearsals and performances, shaping artistic interpretation and technical quality. Composers create original musical works for concert performance, film, television, theater, and other media. Both roles require advanced musical knowledge and the ability to communicate artistic vision to performers.

title: Musicians and Singers
description: Musicians and Singers perform vocal or instrumental music for live audiences or in recording studios across a broad range of genres and settings. They rehearse extensively, collaborate with fellow performers and producers, and develop artistic identities that distinguish them in competitive music markets. Their performances entertain, inspire, and contribute to cultural expression.

title: Disc Jockeys, Except Radio
description: Disc Jockeys select and play recorded music at live events, clubs, private parties, and other entertainment venues to create engaging and appropriate atmospheres for audiences. They mix tracks, manage sound levels, and read crowd energy to curate dynamic playlists and keep audiences entertained. Their technical skills with DJ equipment and musical knowledge are central to their performance.

title: Broadcast Announcers and Radio Disc Jockeys
description: Broadcast Announcers and Radio Disc Jockeys present music, news, sports, weather, and commentary over radio and television, engaging audiences through compelling on-air personalities. They introduce content, conduct interviews, and often contribute to the editorial direction of programs. Their vocal delivery, media presence, and ability to connect with audiences are key to their professional success.

title: News Analysts, Reporters, and Journalists
description: News Analysts, Reporters, and Journalists gather, investigate, and present information on current events for newspapers, magazines, television, radio, and digital media. They conduct interviews, research stories, and produce accurate and timely reports that inform the public on issues of local, national, and global significance. Journalistic integrity, strong writing, and source development are core to their practice.

title: Public Relations Specialists
description: Public Relations Specialists create and maintain a favorable public image for organizations and individuals by managing media relations, crafting press materials, and developing communications strategies. They respond to media inquiries, coordinate press events, and help shape narratives in ways that align with organizational goals and values. Effective PR requires strong writing, media knowledge, and strategic communication skills.

title: Editors
description: Editors review, revise, and oversee written content for publications, websites, and other media to ensure accuracy, clarity, and stylistic consistency. They work with writers to improve drafts, fact-check content, and ensure alignment with editorial standards and audience expectations. Their role spans acquisitions, developmental editing, copy editing, and production oversight.

title: Technical Writers
description: Technical Writers produce clear, accurate documentation that explains complex technical processes, products, and systems to intended audiences. They create user manuals, help guides, API documentation, and instructional materials by working closely with subject matter experts and development teams. Their ability to translate technical language into accessible content is essential across technology, engineering, and healthcare industries.

title: Writers and Authors
description: Writers and Authors develop original written content across a wide range of formats including books, articles, screenplays, and online content for entertainment, information, and commercial purposes. They research topics, develop ideas, and craft narratives or informational pieces that engage and inform target audiences. Building a sustainable writing career often requires both creative skill and entrepreneurial self-promotion.

title: Poets, Lyricists and Creative Writers
description: Poets, Lyricists, and Creative Writers produce artistic written works including poetry, song lyrics, fiction, and experimental prose that express ideas, emotions, and experiences through language. They develop distinctive voices and styles, often publishing through literary journals, books, music albums, or digital platforms. Their creative output enriches cultural life and contributes to literary and musical traditions.

title: Interpreters and Translators
description: Interpreters and Translators convert spoken or written content from one language to another while preserving meaning, tone, and cultural nuance. Interpreters work in real-time settings such as conferences, courts, and medical appointments, while translators focus on written documents. Fluency in at least two languages and deep subject matter knowledge are fundamental requirements.

title: Court Reporters and Simultaneous Captioners
description: Court Reporters and Simultaneous Captioners create verbatim transcripts of spoken proceedings in legal, legislative, and broadcast settings. They use stenographic machines or voice writing technology to capture spoken words accurately and in real time. Their records serve as official documentation of legal proceedings and accessibility tools for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

title: Audio and Video Technicians
description: Audio and Video Technicians set up, operate, and maintain equipment used to record, amplify, mix, and reproduce sound and video for events, broadcasts, and productions. They troubleshoot technical issues and adjust equipment settings to ensure optimal audio and visual quality for live and recorded content. Their technical expertise supports concerts, conferences, film productions, and broadcast studios.

title: Broadcast Technicians
description: Broadcast Technicians install, operate, and maintain electronic equipment used to transmit radio and television programs. They monitor signal quality, perform equipment maintenance, and resolve technical issues to ensure reliable and uninterrupted broadcast operations. Their technical proficiency supports both live and pre-recorded broadcast productions.

title: Sound Engineering Technicians
description: Sound Engineering Technicians operate equipment to record, mix, synchronize, and reproduce sound for music, film, television, theater, and live events. They work closely with producers, directors, and performers to achieve desired audio quality and ensure technical consistency throughout production. Their expertise in acoustics and digital audio workstations is central to professional audio production.

title: Lighting Technicians
description: Lighting Technicians install, operate, and maintain lighting equipment for film, television, theater, concerts, and live events. They work from lighting designs created by directors and lighting designers, rigging equipment and programming control systems to achieve the required visual effects. Their technical skill and safety awareness are essential to creating impactful and safe production environments.

title: Photographers
description: Photographers use cameras and photographic techniques to capture images for artistic, commercial, journalistic, and scientific purposes. They manage composition, lighting, and equipment settings during shoots and use post-processing software to edit and deliver final images. Photographers may specialize in portraiture, commercial, editorial, wedding, or fine art photography.

title: Camera Operators, Television, Video, and Film
description: Camera Operators capture moving images for television, film, and video productions using professional camera equipment. They work under the direction of directors of photography or directors to achieve desired visual effects, framing, and movement that serve the story. Technical precision and the ability to work seamlessly within large production crews are essential.

title: Film and Video Editors
description: Film and Video Editors assemble recorded footage into a coherent, polished final product for film, television, or digital distribution. They work closely with directors and producers to select the best takes, structure narrative flow, and incorporate sound, music, and visual effects. Their creative and technical mastery of editing software and storytelling is fundamental to the post-production process.

title: Chiropractors
description: Chiropractors diagnose and treat neuromuscular disorders with an emphasis on manual adjustment and manipulation of the spine. They evaluate patients through physical examinations and diagnostic imaging to identify misalignments and develop treatment plans to improve function and reduce pain. Their patient-centered approach integrates manual therapy with lifestyle and wellness counseling.

title: Dentists, General
description: General Dentists diagnose and treat problems affecting patients' teeth, gums, and related oral structures while promoting dental health and disease prevention. They perform procedures such as fillings, extractions, and crowns, and provide guidance on oral hygiene to maintain long-term oral health. Building trusting relationships with patients is key to encouraging regular preventive care.

title: Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons
description: Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeons perform surgical procedures on the mouth, jaw, face, and neck to treat injuries, diseases, and defects. They manage complex cases including wisdom tooth extraction, jaw reconstruction, facial trauma, and tumor removal in both hospital and outpatient settings. Their dual training in dentistry and medicine equips them for highly specialized surgical care.

title: Orthodontists
description: Orthodontists diagnose and correct misalignments of teeth and jaws using appliances such as braces, aligners, and retainers. They develop individualized treatment plans to improve patients' oral function, aesthetics, and long-term dental health. Their specialized training beyond general dentistry focuses on the biomechanics of tooth movement and facial development.

title: Prosthodontists
description: Prosthodontists specialize in restoring and replacing teeth using prosthetic devices such as crowns, bridges, dentures, and dental implants. They treat patients with missing, damaged, or severely worn teeth and complex oral conditions, often coordinating with other dental specialists. Their expertise restores oral function, aesthetics, and patient quality of life.

title: Dietitians and Nutritionists
description: Dietitians and Nutritionists assess nutritional needs and develop personalized eating plans to promote health, manage chronic diseases, and support recovery. They work in hospitals, clinics, community settings, and private practice, counseling patients and developing evidence-based dietary programs. Their expertise spans clinical nutrition, food science, and public health.

title: Optometrists
description: Optometrists examine the eyes and visual system to diagnose vision problems, eye diseases, and related health conditions. They prescribe corrective lenses, provide pre- and post-operative care, and treat certain eye conditions using medications and therapeutic procedures. Their role is central to maintaining visual health and detecting systemic diseases with ocular manifestations.

title: Pharmacists
description: Pharmacists dispense prescription medications, counsel patients on drug use and interactions, and collaborate with healthcare providers to optimize medication therapy. They review prescriptions for accuracy and safety, manage pharmacy operations, and play a growing role in chronic disease management and patient wellness programs. Their pharmaceutical expertise is essential to safe and effective patient care.

title: Physician Assistants
description: Physician Assistants practice medicine under the supervision of physicians by examining patients, diagnosing conditions, ordering tests, and prescribing treatments. They work across a broad range of specialties in hospitals, clinics, and other healthcare settings, providing essential primary and specialty care. Their advanced clinical training enables them to manage many routine and complex patient care needs.

title: Anesthesiologist Assistants
description: Anesthesiologist Assistants work under the direction of licensed anesthesiologists to administer anesthesia and provide perioperative patient care. They assist in developing anesthesia care plans, monitor patients during procedures, and manage anesthesia-related equipment in surgical and procedural settings. Their specialized training ensures patient safety and comfort throughout medical procedures requiring anesthesia.

title: Podiatrists
description: Podiatrists diagnose and treat conditions affecting the foot, ankle, and lower extremities including fractures, infections, deformities, and chronic conditions such as diabetic foot disease. They perform surgical and non-surgical procedures, prescribe orthotics and medications, and provide preventive foot care. Their specialized expertise is critical to mobility and quality of life for patients with lower extremity conditions.

title: Occupational Therapists
description: Occupational Therapists help patients develop, recover, or maintain the skills needed for daily living and working following injury, illness, or disability. They assess functional abilities, design individualized intervention plans, and use purposeful activities and adaptive equipment to improve independence and quality of life. Their holistic approach addresses physical, cognitive, and psychosocial factors affecting daily function.

title: Low Vision Therapists, Orientation and Mobility Specialists, and Vision Rehabilitation Therapists
description: These specialists work with individuals who have visual impairments to maximize their functional independence and safety in daily life. Low Vision Therapists optimize the use of remaining sight through devices and techniques, Orientation and Mobility Specialists teach safe travel skills, and Vision Rehabilitation Therapists provide comprehensive training for independent living. Their collaborative, patient-centered care supports adaptation to vision loss.

title: Physical Therapists
description: Physical Therapists diagnose and treat movement dysfunction and physical impairments caused by injury, illness, or disability using exercise, manual therapy, and modalities. They develop individualized rehabilitation programs to restore function, reduce pain, and prevent further injury across diverse patient populations. Their goal-oriented care helps patients regain mobility and return to meaningful activities.

title: Radiation Therapists
description: Radiation Therapists administer radiation treatments prescribed by radiation oncologists to patients with cancer and other diseases. They operate sophisticated linear accelerators and imaging equipment, position patients precisely, and monitor patient responses throughout the course of treatment. Their technical expertise and compassionate care are central to effective and safe radiation therapy delivery.

title: Recreational Therapists
description: Recreational Therapists use leisure activities such as arts and crafts, sports, music, and community outings to improve and maintain the physical, cognitive, and emotional well-being of patients with disabilities or illness. They assess patient needs, develop therapeutic recreation plans, and document outcomes in clinical settings including hospitals, rehabilitation centers, and long-term care facilities. Their work promotes functional independence and quality of life.

title: Respiratory Therapists
description: Respiratory Therapists assess, treat, and provide care for patients with breathing disorders and cardiopulmonary conditions such as asthma, COPD, and respiratory failure. They manage mechanical ventilators, administer aerosolized medications, and perform diagnostic pulmonary function tests in hospitals, clinics, and home care settings. Their specialized expertise is critical in intensive care and emergency medical environments.

title: Speech-Language Pathologists
description: Speech-Language Pathologists assess, diagnose, and treat communication and swallowing disorders in patients across all age groups. They develop individualized therapy programs to address conditions such as stuttering, aphasia, voice disorders, and dysphagia resulting from stroke, brain injury, developmental delays, or other causes. Their evidence-based interventions restore communication abilities and support patient quality of life.

title: Exercise Physiologists
description: Assess, plan, or implement fitness programs that include exercise or physical activities such as those designed to improve cardiorespiratory function, body composition, muscular strength, and endurance.

title: Art Therapists
description: Plan or conduct art therapy sessions or programs to improve clients' physical, cognitive, or emotional well-being.

title: Music Therapists
description: Plan, organize, or direct music therapy programs designed to influence physical, psychological, or behavioral status.

title: Veterinarians
description: Diagnose, treat, or research diseases and injuries of animals. Includes veterinarians who conduct research and development.

title: Registered Nurses
description: Assess patient health problems and needs, develop and implement nursing care plans, and maintain medical records. Administer nursing care to ill, injured, convalescent, or disabled patients.

title: Acute Care Nurses
description: Provide advanced nursing care for acute and critically ill patients.

title: Advanced Practice Psychiatric Nurses
description: Provide advanced nursing care for patients with psychiatric disorders. May provide psychotherapy under the direction of a psychiatrist.

title: Critical Care Nurses
description: Provide advanced nursing care for patients in critical or coronary care units.

title: Clinical Nurse Specialists
description: Provide advanced nursing care and clinical expertness in a specialized area of nursing, such as gerontology, pediatrics, or psychiatric care.

title: Nurse Anesthetists
description: Administer anesthesia, monitor patient's vital signs, and oversee patient recovery from anesthesia. May assist anesthesiologists, surgeons, and other medical professionals.

title: Nurse Midwives
description: Diagnose and coordinate all aspects of the birthing process, either independently or as part of a healthcare team.

title: Nurse Practitioners
description: Diagnose and treat acute, episodic, or chronic illness, independently or as part of a healthcare team. May focus on health promotion and disease prevention.

title: Audiologists
description: Assess and treat persons with hearing and related disorders. May fit hearing aids and provide auditory training.

title: Anesthesiologists
description: Administer anesthetics prior to, during, or after surgery or other medical procedures.

title: Cardiologists
description: Diagnose, treat, manage, and prevent diseases or conditions of the cardiovascular system. May further subspecialize in interventional procedures, echocardiography, or electrophysiology.

title: Dermatologists
description: Diagnose and treat diseases relating to the skin, hair, and nails. May perform both medical and dermatological surgery functions.

title: Emergency Medicine Physicians
description: Make immediate medical decisions and act to prevent death or further disability. Provide immediate recognition, evaluation, care, stabilization, and disposition of a generally diversified population of adult and pediatric patients.

title: Family Medicine Physicians
description: Diagnose, treat, and provide preventive care to individuals and families across the lifespan. May manage common and complex illnesses.

title: General Internal Medicine Physicians
description: Diagnose and provide non-surgical treatment of diseases and injuries of internal organ systems. Provide care mainly for adults who have a wide range of problems associated with the internal organs.

title: Neurologists
description: Diagnose, treat, and prevent diseases or conditions of the nervous system.

title: Obstetricians and Gynecologists
description: Provide medical care related to pregnancy or childbirth and diagnose, treat, and help prevent diseases of women, particularly those affecting the reproductive system.

title: Pediatricians, General
description: Diagnose, treat, and help prevent children's diseases and injuries.

title: Physicians, Pathologists
description: Diagnose diseases and conduct lab tests using organs, body tissues, and fluids.

title: Psychiatrists
description: Diagnose, treat, and help prevent disorders of the mind. Includes psychoanalysts.

title: Radiologists
description: Examine and diagnose disorders and diseases using x-rays and radioactive materials. May treat patients.

title: Allergists and Immunologists
description: Diagnose, treat, and help prevent allergic diseases and disease processes affecting the immune system.

title: Hospitalists
description: Provide inpatient medical care to patients in a hospital setting.

title: Urologists
description: Diagnose, treat, and help prevent diseases of the urinary tract and male reproductive system.

title: Physical Medicine and Rehabilitation Physicians
description: Diagnose and treat disorders requiring physiotherapy to provide physical, mental, and occupational rehabilitation.

title: Preventive Medicine Physicians
description: Plan, implement, and evaluate health programs to promote health and prevent disease.

title: Sports Medicine Physicians
description: Diagnose, treat, and help prevent injuries that occur during sporting events, athletic training, and physical activities.

title: Ophthalmologists, Except Pediatric
description: Diagnose, treat, and help prevent diseases and injuries of the eyes and related structures.

title: Orthopedic Surgeons, Except Pediatric
description: Diagnose, treat, and help prevent diseases and injuries of the musculoskeletal system.

title: Pediatric Surgeons
description: Diagnose and perform surgery to treat fetal abnormalities and birth defects, diseases, and injuries in children.

title: Acupuncturists
description: Provide treatment of symptoms and disorders using needles and small electrical currents.

title: Dental Hygienists
description: Clean teeth and examine oral areas, head, and neck for signs of oral disease. May educate patients on oral hygiene.

title: Naturopathic Physicians
description: Diagnose, treat, and help prevent diseases using a system of practice that is based on the natural healing capacity of individuals.

title: Orthoptists
description: Diagnose and treat visual system disorders such as binocular vision and eye movement anomalies.

title: Medical and Clinical Laboratory Technologists
description: Perform complex medical laboratory tests for diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of disease.

title: Cytogenetic Technologists
description: Analyze chromosomes found in biological specimens such as amniotic fluids, bone marrow, and blood.

title: Cytotechnologists
description: Stain, mount, and study cells to detect evidence of cancer, hormonal abnormalities, and other pathological conditions.

title: Histotechnologists
description: Prepare histologic slides from tissue sections for microscopic examination and diagnosis by pathologists.

title: Medical and Clinical Laboratory Technicians
description: Perform routine medical laboratory tests for the diagnosis, treatment, and prevention of disease.

title: Histology Technicians
description: Prepare histologic slides from tissue sections for microscopic examination and diagnosis by pathologists.

title: Cardiovascular Technologists and Technicians
description: Conduct tests on pulmonary or cardiovascular systems of patients for diagnostic purposes.

title: Diagnostic Medical Sonographers
description: Produce ultrasonic recordings of internal organs for use by physicians.

title: Nuclear Medicine Technologists
description: Prepare, administer, and measure radioactive isotopes in therapeutic, diagnostic, and tracer studies.

title: Radiologic Technologists and Technicians
description: Take x-rays and CAT scans or administer nonradioactive materials into patient's blood stream for diagnostic purposes.

title: Magnetic Resonance Imaging Technologists
description: Operate Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI) scanners.

title: Medical Dosimetrists
description: Generate radiation therapy treatment plans under the direction of an oncologist.

title: Emergency Medical Technicians
description: Assess injuries, administer emergency medical care, and extricate trapped individuals.

title: Paramedics
description: Administer basic or advanced emergency medical care and assess injuries and illnesses.

title: Dietetic Technicians
description: Assist in the provision of food service and nutritional programs, under the supervision of a dietitian.

title: Pharmacy Technicians
description: Prepare medications under the direction of a pharmacist.

title: Psychiatric Technicians
description: Care for individuals with mental or emotional conditions or disabilities, following the instructions of physicians or other health practitioners.

title: Surgical Technologists
description: Assist in operations, under the supervision of surgeons, registered nurses, or other surgical personnel.

title: Veterinary Technologists and Technicians
description: Perform medical tests in a laboratory environment for use in the treatment and diagnosis of diseases in animals.

title: Ophthalmic Medical Technicians
description: Assist ophthalmologists by performing ophthalmic clinical functions.

title: Licensed Practical and Licensed Vocational Nurses
description: Care for ill, injured, or convalescing patients or persons with disabilities in hospitals, nursing homes, clinics, private homes, group homes, and similar institutions.

title: Medical Records Specialists
description: Compile, process, and maintain medical records of hospital and clinic patients in a manner consistent with medical, administrative, ethical, legal, and regulatory requirements.

title: Opticians, Dispensing
description: Design, measure, fit, and adapt lenses and frames for client according to written optical prescription or specification.

title: Orthotists and Prosthetists
description: Design, measure, fit, and adapt orthopedic braces, appliances or prostheses.

title: Hearing Aid Specialists
description: Select and fit hearing aids for customers. Administer and interpret tests of hearing.

title: Neurodiagnostic Technologists
description: Conduct electroneurodiagnostic (END) tests such as electroencephalograms, evoked potentials, polysomnograms, or electronystagmograms.

title: Ophthalmic Medical Technologists
description: Assist ophthalmologists by performing ophthalmic clinical functions and ophthalmic photography.

title: Patient Representatives
description: Assist patients in obtaining services, understanding policies and making health care decisions.

title: Health Information Technologists and Medical Registrars
description: Apply knowledge of health care and information systems to assist in the design, development, and ongoing modification of computerized health care systems.

title: Athletic Trainers
description: Evaluate and advise individuals to assist recovery from or avoid athletic-related injuries or illnesses, or maintain peak physical fitness.

title: Genetic Counselors
description: Assess individual or family risk for a variety of inherited conditions, such as genetic disorders and birth defects.

title: Surgical Assistants
description: Assist in operations, under the supervision of surgeons. May, in accordance with state laws, help surgeons to make incisions and close surgical sites.

title: Midwives
description: Demonstrate and teach care to patients, and assist them during pregnancy and childbirth.

title: Home Health Aides
description: Provide routine individualized healthcare such as changing bandages and dressing wounds, and applying topical medications to the elderly, convalescents, or persons with disabilities at the patient's home.

title: Personal Care Aides
description: Assist the elderly, convalescents, or persons with disabilities with daily living activities at the person's home or in a care facility.

title: Nursing Assistants
description: Provide basic patient care under direction of nursing staff. Perform duties such as feed, bathe, dress, groom, or move patients.

title: Orderlies
description: Transport patients to areas such as operating rooms or x-ray rooms using wheelchairs, stretchers, or moveable beds.

title: Psychiatric Aides
description: Assist mentally impaired or emotionally disturbed patients, working under direction of nursing and medical staff.

title: Occupational Therapy Assistants
description: Assist occupational therapists in providing occupational therapy treatments and procedures. May, in accordance with state laws, assist in development of treatment plans.

title: Occupational Therapy Aides
description: Prepare patient and treatment room for therapy under direction of occupational therapist. May perform clerical duties.

title: Physical Therapist Assistants
description: Assist physical therapists in providing physical therapy treatments and procedures. May assist in the development of treatment plans.

title: Physical Therapist Aides
description: Prepare patient and treatment area for therapy. Perform clerical duties and assist physical therapists as needed.

title: Massage Therapists
description: Perform therapeutic massages of soft tissues and joints. May assist in the assessment of range of motion and muscle strength.

title: Dental Assistants
description: Assist dentist, set up equipment, prepare patient for treatment, and keep records.

title: Medical Assistants
description: Perform administrative and certain clinical duties under the direction of a physician. Schedule appointments, maintain records, take vitals, and assist during examinations.

title: Medical Equipment Preparers
description: Prepare, sterilize, install, or clean laboratory or healthcare equipment. May perform routine laboratory tasks and operate or inspect equipment.

title: Medical Transcriptionists
description: Transcribe medical reports recorded by physicians and other healthcare practitioners using various electronic devices.

title: Pharmacy Aides
description: Record drugs delivered to the pharmacy, store incoming merchandise, and inform the supervisor of stock needs. May operate cash register and accept prescriptions.

title: Veterinary Assistants and Laboratory Animal Caretakers
description: Feed, water, and examine pets and other nonfarm animals for signs of illness, disease, or injury in laboratories, animal hospitals, and clinics.

title: Phlebotomists
description: Draw blood for tests, transfusions, donations, or research. May explain the procedure to patients and assist in the recovery of patients with adverse reactions.

title: Speech-Language Pathology Assistants
description: Assist speech-language pathologists in the assessment and treatment of speech, language, voice, and fluency disorders.

title: Endoscopy Technicians
description: Assist physicians and nurses performing endoscopies. Prepare and maintain instruments and equipment.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Correctional Officers
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of correctional officers and jailers.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Police and Detectives
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of members of police force.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Firefighting and Prevention Workers
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in firefighting and fire prevention and control.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Security Workers
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of security workers and security guards.

title: Firefighters
description: Control and extinguish fires or respond to emergency situations where life, property, or the environment is at risk.

title: Fire Inspectors and Investigators
description: Inspect buildings to detect fire hazards and enforce local ordinances and state laws, or investigate and gather facts to determine cause of fires and explosions.

title: Forest Fire Inspectors and Prevention Specialists
description: Enforce fire regulations, inspect forest areas for fire hazards, and recommend forest fire prevention or control measures.

title: Bailiffs
description: Maintain order in courts of law.

title: Correctional Officers and Jailers
description: Guard inmates in penal or rehabilitative institutions in accordance with established regulations and procedures.

title: Detectives and Criminal Investigators
description: Conduct investigations related to suspected violations of federal, state, or local laws to prevent or solve crimes.

title: Police Identification and Records Officers
description: Collect evidence at crime scenes, classify and identify fingerprints, and photograph evidence for use in criminal and civil cases.

title: Intelligence Analysts
description: Gather, analyze, or evaluate information from a variety of sources to anticipate and prevent organized crime or terrorism.

title: Fish and Game Wardens
description: Patrol assigned area to prevent fish and game law violations. Investigate reports of damage to crops or property by wildlife.

title: Parking Enforcement Workers
description: Patrol assigned area to enforce motor vehicle parking regulations.

title: Police and Sheriff's Patrol Officers
description: Maintain order and protect life and property by enforcing local, tribal, state, or federal laws and ordinances.

title: Customs and Border Protection Officers
description: Enforce customs, immigration, and agriculture law and regulations at U.S. ports of entry and preclearance locations worldwide.

title: Transit and Railroad Police
description: Protect and police railroad and transit property, employees, or passengers.

title: Animal Control Workers
description: Handle animals for the purpose of investigations of mistreatment, or control of abandoned, dangerous, or unattended animals.

title: Private Detectives and Investigators
description: Gather, analyze, compile, and report information regarding individuals or organizations to clients, or detect occurrences of unlawful acts or infractions of rules in private establishments.

title: Gambling Surveillance Officers and Gambling Investigators
description: Observe gambling operation for irregular activities such as cheating or theft by either employees or patrons.

title: Security Guards
description: Guard, patrol, or monitor premises to prevent theft, violence, or infractions of rules.

title: Crossing Guards and Flaggers
description: Guide or control vehicular or pedestrian traffic at such places as streets, schools, railroad crossings, or construction sites.

title: Lifeguards, Ski Patrol, and Other Recreational Protective Service Workers
description: Monitor recreational areas, such as pools, beaches, or ski slopes, to provide assistance and protection to participants.

title: Transportation Security Screeners
description: Conduct screening of passengers, baggage, or cargo to ensure compliance with Transportation Security Administration (TSA) regulations.

title: School Bus Monitors
description: Maintain order among students on a school bus and assist students in safely boarding and exiting.

title: Retail Loss Prevention Specialists
description: Implement procedures and systems to prevent merchandise loss in retail environments. Conduct audits and investigations of employee and customer theft.

title: Chefs and Head Cooks
description: Direct and may participate in the preparation, seasoning, and cooking of salads, soups, fish, meats, vegetables, desserts, or other foods.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Food Preparation and Serving Workers
description: Supervise workers engaged in preparing and serving food.

title: Cooks, Fast Food
description: Prepare and cook food in a fast food restaurant with a limited menu.

title: Cooks, Institution and Cafeteria
description: Prepare and cook large quantities of food for institutions, such as schools, hospitals, or cafeterias.

title: Cooks, Private Household
description: Prepare meals in private homes according to employers' recipes or tastes, handling all meals for the family and possibly for other household staff.

title: Cooks, Restaurant
description: Prepare, season, and cook dishes such as soups, meats, vegetables, or desserts in restaurants.

title: Cooks, Short Order
description: Prepare and cook to order a variety of foods that require only a short preparation time.

title: Food Preparation Workers
description: Perform a variety of food preparation duties other than cooking, such as preparing cold foods and slicing meat.

title: Bartenders
description: Mix and serve drinks to patrons, directly or through waitstaff.

title: Fast Food and Counter Workers
description: Take orders, serve food and beverages, and process payments at fast food or quick-service restaurants.

title: Baristas
description: Prepare and serve specialty coffee and other beverages to customers.

title: Waiters and Waitresses
description: Take orders and serve food and beverages to patrons at tables in dining establishment.

title: Food Servers, Nonrestaurant
description: Serve food to individuals outside of a restaurant environment, such as in hotel rooms, hospital rooms, or cars.

title: Dining Room and Cafeteria Attendants and Bartender Helpers
description: Facilitate food service. Clean tables, remove dirty dishes, replace soiled table linens, set tables, and replenish supply of clean linens, silverware, and glassware.

title: Dishwashers
description: Clean dishes, kitchen, food preparation equipment, or utensils.

title: Hosts and Hostesses, Restaurant, Lounge, and Coffee Shop
description: Welcome patrons, seat them at tables or in lounge, and help ensure quality of facilities and service.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Housekeeping and Janitorial Workers
description: Supervise work activities of cleaning personnel in hotels, hospitals, offices, and other establishments.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Landscaping, Lawn Service, and Groundskeeping Workers
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of workers engaged in landscaping or groundskeeping activities.

title: Janitors and Cleaners, Except Maids and Housekeeping Cleaners
description: Keep buildings in clean and orderly condition. Perform heavy cleaning duties, such as cleaning floors, washing walls and glass, and removing rubbish.

title: Maids and Housekeeping Cleaners
description: Perform any combination of light cleaning duties to maintain private households or commercial establishments, such as hotels and hospitals, in a clean and orderly manner.

title: Pest Control Workers
description: Apply or release chemical solutions or toxic gases and set traps to kill or route pests and vermin that infest buildings and surrounding areas.

title: Landscaping and Groundskeeping Workers
description: Landscape or maintain grounds of property using hand or power tools or equipment. Tasks may include mowing, trimming, planting, watering, and fertilizing.

title: Pesticide Handlers, Sprayers, and Applicators, Vegetation
description: Mix or apply pesticides, herbicides, fungicides, or insecticides through sprays, dusts, vapors, soil incorporation, or chemical application on trees, shrubs, and lawns.

title: Tree Trimmers and Pruners
description: Cut away dead or excess branches from trees or shrubs to maintain right-of-way for roads, sidewalks, or utilities, or to improve appearance, health, and value of tree.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Gambling Services Workers
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of workers in assigned gaming areas.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Entertainment and Recreation Workers, Except Gambling Services
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of entertainment and recreation related workers.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Personal Service Workers
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of personal service workers, such as flight attendants, hairdressers, or caddies.

title: Animal Trainers
description: Train animals for riding, harness, security, performance, or obedience, or for assisting persons with disabilities.

title: Animal Caretakers
description: Feed, water, groom, bathe, exercise, or otherwise care for pets and other nonfarm animals, such as dogs, cats, ornamental fish or birds, zoo animals, and mice.

title: Gambling Dealers
description: Operate table games. Stand or sit behind table and operate games of chance by dispensing the appropriate number of cards or blocks to players, or operating other gaming equipment.

title: Gambling and Sports Book Writers and Runners
description: Post information with respect to sporting events or games of chance. Receive, verify, and record patrons' wagers.

title: Motion Picture Projectionists
description: Set up and operate motion picture projection and related sound reproduction equipment.

title: Ushers, Lobby Attendants, and Ticket Takers
description: Assist patrons at entertainment events by performing duties, such as collecting admission tickets and passes from patrons, assisting in finding seats, searching for lost articles, and locating such facilities as rest rooms and telephones.

title: Amusement and Recreation Attendants
description: Perform variety of attending duties at amusement or recreation facility. May schedule use of recreation facilities, maintain and provide equipment to participants of sporting events or recreational pursuits, or operate amusement concessions and rides.

title: Costume Attendants
description: Costume Attendants organize, maintain, and distribute costumes and related accessories for theatrical, film, television, and other performance productions. They assist performers with dressing, perform minor repairs, and ensure costumes are clean and ready for each performance or shoot. Their behind-the-scenes support is essential to the smooth execution of productions requiring wardrobe management.

title: Locker Room, Coatroom, and Dressing Room Attendants
description: Locker Room, Coatroom, and Dressing Room Attendants provide personal assistance and supervision services in facilities such as gyms, clubs, theaters, and event venues. They issue and collect towels, manage stored belongings, and maintain cleanliness and order in assigned areas. Their attentive customer service contributes to a comfortable and organized guest experience.

title: Embalmers
description: Embalmers prepare deceased individuals for burial or viewing by treating and preserving the body using chemical solutions and restorative techniques. They disinfect and sanitize remains, apply cosmetics, and arrange the body to present a dignified appearance for family viewings. Their work requires technical skill, sensitivity, and strict adherence to health and safety regulations.

title: Crematory Operators
description: Crematory Operators manage and operate cremation equipment to process human remains in accordance with legal, ethical, and facility standards. They maintain accurate records of each cremation, handle remains with care, and ensure equipment is properly maintained and functioning safely. Their role demands precision, discretion, and respect for families during a sensitive time.

title: Funeral Attendants
description: Funeral Attendants assist in the preparation and execution of funeral services by performing tasks such as directing mourners, arranging flowers, and preparing service spaces. They provide compassionate support to grieving families and ensure that ceremonies proceed respectfully and smoothly. Their attentive service plays an important role in creating a dignified experience during a difficult time.

title: Morticians, Undertakers, and Funeral Arrangers
description: Morticians, Undertakers, and Funeral Arrangers coordinate and manage all aspects of funeral services, working directly with bereaved families to plan ceremonies and handle legal requirements. They arrange transportation of remains, liaise with cemeteries and clergy, and oversee embalming and preparation services. Their professional and empathetic approach helps families navigate end-of-life arrangements with dignity.

title: Barbers
description: Barbers cut, trim, and style hair primarily for male clients, and may also provide services such as shaving, beard grooming, and scalp treatments. They consult with clients to understand desired styles and use professional tools to deliver precise, personalized results. Building lasting client relationships through skilled service and engaging conversation is central to a successful barbering career.

title: Hairdressers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists
description: Hairdressers, Hairstylists, and Cosmetologists provide a wide range of hair and beauty services including cutting, coloring, styling, and chemical treatments for clients of all genders. They consult with clients to assess needs and preferences, recommend products, and stay current with the latest trends and techniques. Building a loyal clientele requires technical excellence and strong interpersonal skills.

title: Makeup Artists, Theatrical and Performance
description: Theatrical and Performance Makeup Artists apply makeup and prosthetics to transform performers for stage, film, television, and live events. They interpret scripts and character requirements to create looks ranging from natural enhancements to dramatic special effects, often working under time pressure and in demanding production environments. Their artistry and technical skill contribute directly to the visual storytelling of productions.

title: Manicurists and Pedicurists
description: Manicurists and Pedicurists care for clients' fingernails and toenails by cleaning, shaping, and applying polish or artificial nail enhancements. They may also provide hand and foot massages and offer nail art services, ensuring sanitation and client comfort throughout. Their attention to detail and customer service skills are key to building a repeat client base.

title: Shampooers
description: Shampooers wash and condition clients' hair in preparation for styling or other salon services, often providing scalp massages as part of the service. They assist hairstylists by keeping workstations clean, preparing clients, and maintaining salon hygiene standards. Their supportive role contributes to a pleasant and efficient salon experience for clients.

title: Skincare Specialists
description: Skincare Specialists, also known as estheticians, provide professional skin care treatments including facials, exfoliation, hair removal, and other therapeutic services to improve and maintain skin health. They assess clients' skin conditions, recommend appropriate treatments and home care products, and keep up with advances in skincare science and techniques. Their personalized approach helps clients achieve and maintain healthy, radiant skin.

title: Baggage Porters and Bellhops
description: Baggage Porters and Bellhops assist guests at hotels, airports, and transportation terminals by transporting luggage and providing information and hospitality services. They escort guests to rooms, explain hotel amenities, and respond to guest requests to enhance the overall hospitality experience. Their courteous, attentive service plays a key role in creating positive first and last impressions for guests.

title: Concierges
description: Concierges provide personalized assistance to hotel guests and building residents by arranging reservations, transportation, entertainment, and other services. They serve as a knowledgeable resource for local attractions, dining, and events, and handle special requests to ensure an exceptional guest experience. Building strong networks with local vendors and service providers is key to excelling in this role.

title: Tour Guides and Escorts
description: Tour Guides and Escorts lead individuals and groups through cultural, historical, and natural attractions, providing informative and engaging commentary. They manage group logistics, respond to questions, and ensure the safety and satisfaction of tour participants throughout the experience. Their knowledge, enthusiasm, and communication skills are central to delivering memorable tours.

title: Travel Guides
description: Travel Guides provide destination expertise and personalized assistance to travelers before and during their journeys, helping them navigate unfamiliar places and cultures. They offer recommendations on accommodations, dining, activities, and local customs to enhance the overall travel experience. Their in-depth destination knowledge and interpersonal skills distinguish them as trusted resources for travelers.

title: Childcare Workers
description: Childcare Workers attend to the needs of infants, toddlers, and young children in daycare centers, preschools, and private homes, ensuring their safety, development, and well-being. They organize age-appropriate activities, assist with meals and hygiene, and communicate regularly with parents about children's progress. Their nurturing, attentive care provides a safe and stimulating environment for early childhood development.

title: Nannies
description: Nannies provide in-home childcare for families, taking responsibility for the daily care, education, and supervision of children in a private household setting. They may manage children's schedules, assist with homework, prepare meals, and transport children to activities while forming close, trusted relationships with families. Their role often requires a higher degree of autonomy and child development expertise than general childcare.

title: Exercise Trainers and Group Fitness Instructors
description: Exercise Trainers and Group Fitness Instructors lead individuals and groups in physical exercise programs designed to improve strength, endurance, flexibility, and overall health. They assess clients' fitness levels, design personalized or group workout plans, demonstrate techniques, and motivate participants to achieve their fitness goals safely. Their expertise in exercise science and ability to inspire clients are central to their effectiveness.

title: Recreation Workers
description: Recreation Workers plan, organize, and lead recreational activities and programs for individuals of all ages in community centers, parks, summer camps, and similar settings. They facilitate sports, arts, social activities, and therapeutic recreation to promote physical and mental well-being. Their ability to engage diverse populations in meaningful leisure experiences is central to their role.

title: Residential Advisors
description: Residential Advisors oversee and support residents in college dormitories, group homes, or other residential facilities, fostering a safe, inclusive, and supportive living environment. They enforce community policies, organize social and educational programs, and serve as a first point of contact for residents experiencing personal or academic challenges. Their mentoring and conflict resolution skills are essential to maintaining a positive residential community.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Retail Sales Workers
description: First-Line Supervisors of Retail Sales Workers directly oversee the daily activities of retail sales staff, ensuring exceptional customer service and efficient store operations. They assign duties, resolve customer complaints, monitor inventory, and train new employees to meet sales and service standards. Their leadership on the sales floor is critical to achieving store performance targets.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Non-Retail Sales Workers
description: First-Line Supervisors of Non-Retail Sales Workers manage and direct sales teams in non-retail environments such as wholesale, financial services, and outside sales. They set sales targets, monitor individual and team performance, coach staff, and coordinate with other departments to support sales strategies. Their ability to motivate teams and analyze sales data drives revenue growth.

title: Cashiers
description: Cashiers process customer purchases by operating point-of-sale systems, handling payments, and providing receipts in retail, food service, and other customer-facing environments. They greet customers, verify prices, and assist with basic product questions to ensure a smooth and positive checkout experience. Accuracy, efficiency, and friendly service are the hallmarks of effective cashiers.

title: Gambling Change Persons and Booth Cashiers
description: Gambling Change Persons and Booth Cashiers exchange currency for gaming tokens or chips and process payouts for patrons in casinos and gaming establishments. They verify patron identification, maintain accurate cash counts, and adhere to gaming regulations and security protocols. Their accuracy and knowledge of gaming procedures are essential to the integrity of casino financial operations.

title: Counter and Rental Clerks
description: Counter and Rental Clerks assist customers in renting or purchasing products and services at service counters in industries such as vehicle rental, equipment hire, and dry cleaning. They process transactions, explain rental agreements, and inspect returned items for damage or discrepancies. Their customer-facing role requires product knowledge, attention to detail, and strong service skills.

title: Parts Salespersons
description: Parts Salespersons assist customers in identifying and purchasing the correct components for vehicles, machinery, and equipment in retail or wholesale settings. They use parts catalogs and computerized inventory systems to locate items, provide pricing information, and process orders. Technical product knowledge and strong customer service skills are essential to successfully matching customers with the right parts.

title: Retail Salespersons
description: Retail Salespersons assist customers in selecting and purchasing products in stores, showrooms, and other retail environments. They greet customers, explain product features and benefits, process transactions, and handle returns or exchanges to ensure a positive shopping experience. Building rapport with customers and maintaining product knowledge are key to meeting individual and store sales targets.

title: Advertising Sales Agents
description: Advertising Sales Agents sell advertising space and time across print, digital, broadcast, and outdoor media platforms to businesses seeking to promote their products and services. They prospect for new clients, present tailored advertising solutions, negotiate contracts, and manage ongoing client relationships. Their success depends on understanding client marketing objectives and demonstrating the value of their media offerings.

title: Insurance Sales Agents
description: Insurance Sales Agents contact potential clients and sell life, health, property, and casualty insurance products to individuals and businesses. They assess client needs, explain policy options, and help clients select appropriate coverage while managing renewals and policy changes over time. Building a strong book of business requires consistent prospecting, relationship management, and in-depth product knowledge.

title: Securities, Commodities, and Financial Services Sales Agents
description: Securities, Commodities, and Financial Services Sales Agents buy and sell financial products including stocks, bonds, and commodities on behalf of clients or their firms. They advise clients on investment strategies, monitor market activity, and execute trades in compliance with regulatory requirements. Their financial expertise and client relationship skills are central to managing investment portfolios and driving sales.

title: Travel Agents
description: Travel Agents plan and book travel arrangements including flights, hotels, tours, and cruises for individual and corporate clients. They research destinations, compare pricing and itineraries, and provide expert recommendations to create customized travel experiences that meet client preferences and budgets. Their destination knowledge and industry relationships provide value that self-service booking tools cannot always replicate.

title: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
description: Sales Representatives in this category sell a broad range of business and consumer services such as staffing, telecommunications, and logistics to clients across various industries. They identify prospects, present service offerings, negotiate contracts, and maintain client accounts to achieve sales targets. Their consultative approach and ability to communicate value propositions distinguish top performers.

title: Sales Representatives, Wholesale and Manufacturing, Technical and Scientific Products
description: These Sales Representatives sell complex technical and scientific products such as laboratory equipment, industrial machinery, and technology solutions to business and institutional clients. They leverage deep product and industry knowledge to educate clients, address technical questions, and demonstrate how their offerings solve specific operational challenges. Their technical credibility and relationship-building skills drive sales of high-value products.

title: Sales Representatives, Wholesale and Manufacturing, Except Technical and Scientific Products
description: These Sales Representatives sell non-technical goods such as consumer products, food items, and general industrial supplies to retailers, distributors, and other businesses. They manage territory accounts, take orders, promote new products, and build long-term relationships with purchasing contacts. Consistent prospecting and strong product knowledge are essential to maintaining and growing a sales territory.

title: Demonstrators and Product Promoters
description: Demonstrators and Product Promoters showcase products to consumers in retail stores, trade shows, and other venues to generate interest and drive sales. They prepare and deliver live product demonstrations, answer customer questions, and distribute samples to increase brand awareness and purchase intent. Their engaging presentation skills and product enthusiasm directly influence consumer purchasing behavior.

title: Models
description: Models pose for photographs, appear in advertisements, walk in fashion shows, and represent brands and products in various commercial and artistic contexts. They work with photographers, designers, and creative directors to bring visual concepts to life and effectively communicate the intended image of a product or brand. Professional modeling requires physical presentation, adaptability, and the ability to interpret and execute creative direction.

title: Real Estate Brokers
description: Real Estate Brokers facilitate the buying, selling, and renting of properties for clients, managing transactions from initial listing or search through to closing. They supervise real estate agents, ensure legal compliance, negotiate on behalf of clients, and provide expert market analysis. Their licensure, experience, and professional network are central to successfully navigating complex real estate transactions.

title: Real Estate Sales Agents
description: Real Estate Sales Agents assist clients in buying, selling, and renting properties by providing market guidance, arranging viewings, and facilitating negotiations under the supervision of a licensed broker. They market listings, conduct open houses, and coordinate with inspectors, appraisers, and lenders throughout the transaction process. Their local market knowledge and interpersonal skills are key to building a successful client base.

title: Sales Engineers
description: Sales Engineers sell complex technical products and services by combining deep engineering knowledge with professional sales skills to address the specific needs of business clients. They provide technical demonstrations, develop customized proposals, and act as a bridge between clients and internal engineering or product teams. Their dual expertise in technology and sales makes them invaluable for high-value, solution-oriented selling.

title: Telemarketers
description: Telemarketers contact prospective and existing customers by telephone to promote products, services, or charitable causes and solicit sales or donations. They follow scripts and sales guidelines, handle objections, and record contact information and outcomes in customer databases. Their persistence, communication skills, and ability to engage customers remotely are fundamental to outbound sales success.

title: Door-to-Door Sales Workers, News and Street Vendors, and Related Workers
description: Door-to-Door Sales Workers and Street Vendors sell merchandise or services directly to consumers in residential areas, public spaces, and other non-store locations. They approach potential customers, demonstrate or describe their offerings, and complete sales transactions on the spot. Their success depends on persistence, communication skills, and the ability to engage quickly with diverse individuals.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers
description: First-Line Supervisors of Office and Administrative Support Workers manage the daily activities of clerical and administrative staff in offices and organizations. They assign tasks, monitor performance, resolve operational issues, and ensure that administrative processes meet organizational standards. Their leadership ensures efficient workflow and high-quality output from support teams.

title: Switchboard Operators, Including Answering Service
description: Switchboard Operators manage incoming and outgoing telephone calls for organizations, routing calls to the appropriate departments or individuals. They answer general inquiries, take messages, and ensure smooth communication flow within and outside the organization. Their courteous and efficient handling of calls reflects the professional image of the organization.

title: Telephone Operators
description: Telephone Operators assist callers with directory assistance, call connections, and telecommunications services. They provide accurate information, troubleshoot basic call issues, and ensure that callers are connected efficiently and professionally. Their role supports telecommunications networks and customer service operations.

title: Bill and Account Collectors
description: Bill and Account Collectors contact individuals and businesses with overdue accounts to negotiate payment arrangements and recover outstanding balances on behalf of creditors. They maintain records of communication, process payments, and work within the boundaries of debt collection regulations. Their persistence and negotiation skills are key to recovering debts while maintaining professional and legally compliant interactions.

title: Billing and Posting Clerks
description: Billing and Posting Clerks prepare and process invoices, credit memos, and billing statements for customers and clients. They verify billing accuracy, post charges to accounts, and reconcile discrepancies between billing records and payments received. Their precision and attention to detail ensure the accuracy of financial records and timely revenue collection.

title: Bookkeeping, Accounting, and Auditing Clerks
description: Bookkeeping, Accounting, and Auditing Clerks maintain financial records by recording transactions, reconciling accounts, and preparing financial reports for organizations. They use accounting software to enter data, verify figures, and support accountants and managers with accurate financial information. Their role is essential to the integrity and timeliness of organizational financial reporting.

title: Gambling Cage Workers
description: Gambling Cage Workers manage the exchange of cash, chips, and vouchers at casino cage operations, processing financial transactions for patrons and the casino floor. They maintain accurate cash counts, verify patron accounts, and adhere strictly to gaming regulations and internal security procedures. Their precision and knowledge of casino financial protocols are essential to the integrity of casino operations.

title: Payroll and Timekeeping Clerks
description: Payroll and Timekeeping Clerks compile, process, and maintain employee time and payroll records to ensure accurate and timely compensation. They verify attendance and hours worked, calculate wages, deductions, and benefits, and process payroll through HR and payroll software systems. Their accuracy and discretion are critical to employee satisfaction and regulatory compliance.

title: Procurement Clerks
description: Procurement Clerks compile requests for materials, prepare purchase orders, and maintain records of goods purchased and their costs for organizations. They communicate with suppliers, track order status, and resolve discrepancies between purchase orders and deliveries. Their organizational skills and attention to detail support efficient supply chain and purchasing operations.

title: Tellers
description: Tellers process routine financial transactions for bank customers including deposits, withdrawals, and transfers at branch locations. They verify customer identification, count currency accurately, and provide information on bank products and services. Their accuracy, efficiency, and customer service skills are the foundation of a positive branch banking experience.

title: Brokerage Clerks
description: Brokerage Clerks perform administrative and clerical tasks to support securities trading operations, including processing orders, maintaining account records, and computing transaction fees. They assist brokers and financial advisors by preparing reports, verifying trade confirmations, and resolving account discrepancies. Their accuracy and knowledge of financial instruments support the efficient operation of brokerage offices.

title: Correspondence Clerks
description: Correspondence Clerks compose and respond to written communications such as letters, emails, and notifications on behalf of organizations, addressing routine inquiries and complaints. They maintain correspondence records, follow organizational communication guidelines, and ensure timely and accurate responses to clients and stakeholders. Their writing clarity and organizational skills are key to effective external communication.

title: Court, Municipal, and License Clerks
description: Court, Municipal, and License Clerks perform administrative functions for government courts and agencies, including maintaining official records, processing legal documents, and issuing licenses and permits. They assist the public with procedural inquiries, schedule hearings, and ensure compliance with statutory administrative requirements. Their organizational precision and knowledge of government procedures support the effective functioning of public institutions.

title: Credit Authorizers, Checkers, and Clerks
description: Credit Authorizers, Checkers, and Clerks evaluate and process credit applications, verify the accuracy of credit information, and approve or deny credit requests based on established criteria. They review financial histories, confirm applicant details, and flag discrepancies or fraud risks in credit transactions. Their analytical attention to detail safeguards organizations from credit risk and fraud.

title: Customer Service Representatives
description: Customer Service Representatives interact with customers to address inquiries, resolve complaints, process orders, and provide information about products and services across a wide range of industries. They communicate via phone, email, chat, and in person, documenting interactions and escalating complex issues as needed. Their ability to listen, empathize, and solve problems efficiently is fundamental to customer satisfaction and retention.

title: Eligibility Interviewers, Government Programs
description: Eligibility Interviewers assess individuals' eligibility for government assistance programs such as Medicaid, food stamps, and unemployment benefits by conducting interviews and reviewing documentation. They explain program requirements, gather and verify applicant information, and make eligibility determinations in accordance with regulatory guidelines. Their thorough, fair approach ensures that public benefits reach those who qualify.

title: File Clerks
description: File Clerks organize, maintain, and retrieve physical and electronic records and documents to ensure that information is accessible and accurately stored. They classify and label files, enter data into records management systems, and assist staff in locating documents as needed. Their organizational skills and attention to detail are foundational to efficient records management.

title: Hotel, Motel, and Resort Desk Clerks
description: Hotel, Motel, and Resort Desk Clerks serve as the primary point of contact for guests, handling check-in and check-out procedures, reservations, and general inquiries. They process payments, assign rooms, coordinate with housekeeping and maintenance, and resolve guest concerns to ensure a smooth and welcoming stay. Their professionalism and hospitality set the tone for the overall guest experience.

title: Interviewers, Except Eligibility and Loan
description: Interviewers in this category conduct structured interviews for purposes such as market research, census data collection, or public opinion surveys. They follow standardized protocols to collect accurate information from respondents, record responses, and ensure data quality. Their communication skills and adherence to research guidelines are essential to the validity of data collected.

title: Library Assistants, Clerical
