description: Library Assistants perform clerical and support duties in libraries, including checking materials in and out, organizing shelves, and assisting patrons in locating resources. They maintain circulation records, process new acquisitions, and support library staff with administrative tasks. Their customer service and organizational skills contribute to an orderly and welcoming library environment.

title: Loan Interviewers and Clerks
description: Loan Interviewers and Clerks gather and verify financial information from loan applicants to support the mortgage and loan origination process. They review and organize application documents, check for completeness and accuracy, and communicate with applicants and lenders to resolve outstanding items. Their thoroughness and knowledge of lending documentation requirements are essential to efficient loan processing.

title: New Accounts Clerks
description: New Accounts Clerks assist customers in opening new bank or financial accounts by collecting required personal and financial information, verifying identity documents, and entering data into account management systems. They explain account types, fees, and features, and ensure that all regulatory requirements for account opening are satisfied. Their accuracy and customer service skills create a positive first experience for new clients.

title: Order Clerks
description: Order Clerks receive and process customer orders for goods and services, verifying pricing, availability, and delivery details before entering information into order management systems. They communicate with customers, sales staff, and warehouse or fulfillment teams to coordinate timely and accurate order fulfillment. Their organizational skills and attention to detail minimize order errors and support customer satisfaction.

title: Human Resources Assistants, Except Payroll and Timekeeping
description: Human Resources Assistants provide clerical and administrative support to HR departments by maintaining employee records, assisting with recruitment processes, and coordinating onboarding activities. They respond to employee inquiries, process HR documentation, and help ensure compliance with employment policies and procedures. Their organizational skills and discretion support the effective day-to-day operation of human resources functions.

title: Receptionists and Information Clerks
description: Receptionists and Information Clerks serve as the first point of contact for visitors, clients, and callers at offices, healthcare facilities, and other organizations. They greet guests, answer and route phone calls, manage appointment schedules, and provide general information about the organization. Their professionalism and communication skills create a welcoming first impression and support smooth front-office operations.

title: Reservation and Transportation Ticket Agents and Travel Clerks
description: Reservation and Transportation Ticket Agents assist travelers by booking tickets, processing reservations, and providing information on travel schedules, fares, and policies for airlines, rail services, and other transportation providers. They handle itinerary changes, refunds, and special accommodation requests, ensuring a smooth customer experience throughout the booking process. Their attention to detail and service orientation are key to customer satisfaction in the travel sector.

title: Cargo and Freight Agents
description: Cargo and Freight Agents coordinate the movement of goods by air, sea, rail, and road, preparing shipping documentation, tracking shipments, and communicating with carriers and customers. They ensure compliance with customs regulations and shipping standards, resolve logistical issues, and maintain accurate records of cargo movements. Their organizational expertise supports the efficient and compliant flow of goods through supply chains.

title: Freight Forwarders
description: Freight Forwarders act as intermediaries between shippers and transportation carriers, organizing and managing the movement of goods across domestic and international supply chains. They negotiate rates, arrange multimodal transportation, prepare customs documentation, and advise clients on import and export regulations. Their expertise in global logistics and trade compliance is essential to efficient and legally compliant cargo movement.

title: Couriers and Messengers
description: Couriers and Messengers transport documents, packages, and other items between businesses and individuals, ensuring timely and secure delivery. They plan efficient delivery routes, obtain recipient signatures, and maintain records of deliveries made. Their reliability and time management skills are critical to the on-time delivery of sensitive or time-critical materials.

title: Public Safety Telecommunicators
description: Public Safety Telecommunicators receive emergency calls and dispatch police, fire, and emergency medical services in response to 911 and non-emergency requests for assistance. They gather critical information from callers, coordinate multi-agency responses, and maintain communication with field units throughout incidents. Their calm under pressure and rapid decision-making are essential to saving lives and coordinating emergency services.

title: Dispatchers, Except Police, Fire, and Ambulance
description: Dispatchers coordinate the deployment and routing of vehicles, workers, and equipment for service, delivery, and transportation companies. They communicate with drivers and field personnel, monitor job status, and adjust schedules to respond to changes or emergencies. Their logistical coordination and communication skills keep operations running efficiently and on schedule.

title: Meter Readers, Utilities
description: Meter Readers visit residential and commercial properties to collect consumption data from gas, electric, and water meters for utility billing purposes. They record readings, identify potential meter malfunctions or safety hazards, and report discrepancies to utility companies. Their accuracy and thoroughness ensure that customers are billed correctly and that infrastructure issues are identified promptly.

title: Postal Service Clerks
description: Postal Service Clerks work at post office counters to sell stamps and postal products, weigh and price mailings, and assist customers with domestic and international shipping needs. They sort incoming and outgoing mail and packages, maintain postal records, and ensure compliance with postal regulations. Their customer service skills and knowledge of postal procedures are central to efficient post office operations.

title: Postal Service Mail Carriers
description: Postal Service Mail Carriers sort and deliver letters and packages along assigned routes in residential and commercial areas. They collect outgoing mail, obtain signatures for registered or certified items, and provide basic customer service to postal patrons encountered on their routes. Their reliability, physical endurance, and route familiarity are essential to consistent and accurate mail delivery.

title: Postal Service Mail Sorters, Processors, and Processing Machine Operators
description: Postal Service Mail Sorters, Processors, and Processing Machine Operators prepare and sort incoming and outgoing mail and packages at postal processing facilities using automated and manual sorting methods. They operate high-speed sorting machinery, verify addresses and postage, and route items to the appropriate delivery channels. Their speed, accuracy, and equipment proficiency are vital to the throughput of postal operations.

title: Production, Planning, and Expediting Clerks
description: Production, Planning, and Expediting Clerks coordinate and track the flow of materials, work orders, and production schedules to ensure that manufacturing and service operations run on time and to specification. They communicate between departments, monitor inventory levels, and identify and resolve bottlenecks or delays in the production process. Their organizational skills and attention to operational detail are essential to smooth production management.

title: Shipping, Receiving, and Inventory Clerks
description: Verify and maintain records on incoming and outgoing shipments. Prepare items for shipment by assembling, addressing, stamping, and shipping merchandise or material.

title: Weighers, Measurers, Checkers, and Samplers, Recordkeeping
description: Weigh, measure, and check materials, supplies, and equipment for the purpose of keeping relevant records. Primarily handle recordkeeping rather than manual labor.

title: Executive Secretaries and Executive Administrative Assistants
description: Provide high-level administrative support by conducting research, preparing statistical reports, handling information requests, and performing clerical functions such as preparing correspondence.

title: Legal Secretaries and Administrative Assistants
description: Perform secretarial duties using legal terminology, procedures, and documents. Prepare legal papers and correspondence, such as summonses, complaints, motions, and subpoenas.

title: Medical Secretaries and Administrative Assistants
description: Perform secretarial duties using specific knowledge of medical terminology and hospital, clinic, or laboratory procedures. Duties include scheduling appointments and compiling medical records.

title: Secretaries and Administrative Assistants, Except Legal, Medical, and Executive
description: Perform routine clerical and administrative functions such as drafting correspondence, scheduling appointments, organizing and maintaining paper and electronic files, or providing information to callers.

title: Data Entry Keyers
description: Operate data entry device, such as keyboard or photo composing perforator. Duties may include verifying data and preparing materials for printing.

title: Word Processors and Typists
description: Use word processor, computer, or typewriter to type letters, reports, forms, or other material from rough draft, corrected copy, or voice recording.

title: Desktop Publishers
description: Format typescript and graphic elements using computer software to produce publication-ready material.

title: Insurance Claims and Policy Processing Clerks
description: Process new insurance policies, modifications to existing policies, and claims forms. Obtain information from policyholders to verify the accuracy and completeness of information.

title: Mail Clerks and Mail Machine Operators, Except Postal Service
description: Prepare incoming and outgoing mail for distribution. Use machines to fold, insert, or seal mail into envelopes and stamp outgoing mail.

title: Office Clerks, General
description: Perform duties too varied and diverse to be classified in any specific office clerical occupation, requiring knowledge of office systems and procedures.

title: Office Machine Operators, Except Computer
description: Operate one or more of a variety of office machines, such as photocopying, photographic, and duplicating machines.

title: Proofreaders and Copy Markers
description: Read transcript or proof type setup to detect and mark for correction any grammatical, typographical, or compositional errors.

title: Statistical Assistants
description: Compile and compute data according to statistical formulas for use in statistical studies. May perform actuarial computations and compile charts and graphs for use by actuaries.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Farming, Fishing, and Forestry Workers
description: Supervise and coordinate the activities of agricultural, forestry, and aquacultural workers.

title: Agricultural Inspectors
description: Inspect agricultural commodities, processing equipment, and facilities, and fish and logging operations, to ensure compliance with regulations and laws governing health, quality, and safety.

title: Animal Breeders
description: Select and breed animals according to their genealogy, characteristics, and offspring. May require knowledge of artificial insemination techniques and equipment use.

title: Graders and Sorters, Agricultural Products
description: Grade, sort, or classify unprocessed food and other agricultural products by size, weight, color, or condition.

title: Agricultural Equipment Operators
description: Drive and control farm equipment to till soil and to plant, cultivate, and harvest crops. May perform tasks, such as crop baling or hay bucking.

title: Farmworkers and Laborers, Crop, Nursery, and Greenhouse
description: Manually plant, cultivate, and harvest vegetables, fruits, nuts, horticultural specialties, and field crops. Use hand tools, such as shovels, trowels, hoes, tampers, pruning hooks, shears, and knives.

title: Farmworkers, Farm, Ranch, and Aquacultural Animals
description: Attend to live farm, ranch, or aquacultural animals that may include cattle, sheep, swine, goats, horses and other equines, poultry, finfish, shellfish, and bees.

title: Fishing and Hunting Workers
description: Hunt and trap wild animals for human consumption, fur, feed, bait, or other purposes, or catch and gather finfish, shellfish, and other marine life.

title: Forest and Conservation Workers
description: Under supervision, perform manual labor necessary to develop, maintain, or protect areas such as forests, forested areas, woodlands, wetlands, and rangelands.

title: Fallers
description: Use axes or chainsaws to fell trees using knowledge of tree characteristics and cutting techniques to control direction of fall and minimize tree damage.

title: Logging Equipment Operators
description: Drive logging tractor or wheeled tent vehicle to move tree logs to landing or loading area. May operate equipment to load logs onto trucks.

title: Log Graders and Scalers
description: Grade logs or estimate the marketable content or value of logs or pulpwood in sorting yards, millpond, log deck, or similar locations.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Construction Trades and Extraction Workers
description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of construction or extraction workers.

title: Boilermakers
description: Construct, assemble, maintain, and repair stationary steam boilers and boiler house auxiliaries. Align structures or plate sections to assemble boiler frame tanks or vats.

title: Brickmasons and Blockmasons
description: Lay and bind building materials, such as brick, structural tile, concrete block, cinder block, glass block, and terra-cotta block, with mortar and other substances.

title: Stonemasons
description: Build stone structures, such as piers, walls, and abutments. Lay walks, curbstones, or special types of masonry for vats, tanks, and floors.

title: Carpenters
description: Construct, erect, install, or repair structures and fixtures made of wood, such as concrete forms; building frameworks, including partitions, joists, studding, and rafters.

title: Carpet Installers
description: Lay and install carpet from rolls or blocks on floors. Install padding and trim flooring materials.

title: Floor Layers, Except Carpet, Wood, and Hard Tiles
description: Apply blocks, strips, or sheets of shock-absorbing, sound-deadening, or decorative coverings to floors.

title: Floor Sanders and Finishers
description: Scrape and sand wooden floors to smooth surfaces using floor scraper and floor sanding machine, and apply coats of finish.

title: Tile and Stone Setters
description: Apply hard tile, stone, and comparable materials to walls, floors, ceilings, countertops, and roof decks.

title: Cement Masons and Concrete Finishers
description: Smooth and finish surfaces of poured concrete, such as floors, walks, sidewalks, roads, or curbs using a variety of hand and power tools.

title: Terrazzo Workers and Finishers
description: Apply a mixture of cement, sand, pigment, or marble chips to floors, stairways, and cabinet fixtures to fashion decorative and durable surfaces.

title: Construction Laborers
description: Perform tasks involving physical labor at construction sites. May operate hand and power tools of all types.

title: Paving, Surfacing, and Tamping Equipment Operators
description: Operate equipment used for applying concrete, asphalt, or other materials to road beds, parking lots, or airport runways and taxiways.

title: Pile Driver Operators
description: Operate pile drivers mounted on skids, barges, crawler treads, or locomotive cranes to drive pilings for retaining walls, bulkheads, and foundations.

title: Operating Engineers and Other Construction Equipment Operators
description: Operate one or several types of power construction equipment, such as motor graders, bulldozers, scrapers, compressors, pumps, derricks, shovels, tractors, or front-end loaders.

title: Drywall and Ceiling Tile Installers
description: Apply plasterboard or other wallboard to ceilings or interior walls of buildings. Apply or mount acoustical tiles or blocks.

title: Tapers
description: Seal joints between plasterboard or other wallboard to prepare wall surface for painting or papering.

title: Electricians
description: Install, maintain, and repair electrical wiring, equipment, and fixtures. Ensure that work is in accordance with relevant codes.

title: Glaziers
description: Install glass in windows, skylights, store fronts, and display cases, or on surfaces, such as building fronts, interior walls, ceilings, and tabletops.

title: Insulation Workers, Floor, Ceiling, and Wall
description: Line and cover structures with insulating materials. May work with batt, roll, or blown insulation materials.

title: Insulation Workers, Mechanical
description: Apply insulating materials to pipes or ductwork, or other mechanical systems in order to help control and maintain temperature.

title: Painters, Construction and Maintenance
description: Paint walls, equipment, buildings, bridges, and other structural surfaces, using brushes, rollers, and spray guns.

title: Paperhangers
description: Cover interior walls and ceilings of rooms with decorative wallpaper or fabric.

title: Pipelayers
description: Lay pipe for storm or sanitation sewers, drains, and water mains. Perform any combination of the following tasks: grade trenches or culverts, position pipe, or seal joints.

title: Plumbers, Pipefitters, and Steamfitters
description: Assemble, install, alter, and repair pipelines or pipe systems that carry water, steam, air, or other liquids or gases.

title: Plasterers and Stucco Masons
description: Apply interior or exterior plaster, cement, stucco, or similar materials. May also set ornamental plaster.

title: Reinforcing Iron and Rebar Workers
description: Position and secure steel bars or mesh in concrete forms in order to reinforce concrete. Use a variety of fasteners, rod-bending machines, blowtorches, and hand tools.

title: Roofers
description: Cover roofs of structures with shingles, slate, asphalt, aluminum, wood, or related materials. May spray roofs, sidings, and walls with material to bind, seal, insulate, or soundproof.

title: Sheet Metal Workers
description: Fabricate, assemble, install, and repair sheet metal products and equipment, such as ducts, control boxes, drainpipes, and furnace casings.

title: Structural Iron and Steel Workers
description: Raise, place, and unite iron or steel girders, columns, and other structural members to form completed structures or structural frameworks.

title: Solar Photovoltaic Installers
description: Assemble, install, or maintain solar photovoltaic (PV) systems on roofs or other structures in compliance with site assessment and schematics.

title: Helpers--Brickmasons, Blockmasons, Stonemasons, and Tile and Marble Setters
description: Help brickmasons, blockmasons, stonemasons, or tile and marble setters by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools.

title: Helpers--Carpenters
description: Help carpenters by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area.

title: Helpers--Electricians
description: Help electricians by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area.

title: Helpers--Painters, Paperhangers, Plasterers, and Stucco Masons
description: Help painters, paperhangers, plasterers, or stucco masons by performing duties requiring less skill.

title: Helpers--Pipelayers, Plumbers, Pipefitters, and Steamfitters
description: Help plumbers, pipefitters, steamfitters, or pipelayers by performing duties requiring less skill.

title: Helpers--Roofers
description: Help roofers by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include using, supplying, or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area.

title: Construction and Building Inspectors
description: Inspect structures using engineering skills to determine structural soundness and compliance with specifications, building codes, and other regulations.

title: Elevator and Escalator Installers and Repairers
description: Assemble, install, repair, or maintain electric or hydraulic freight or passenger elevators, escalators, or dumbwaiters.

title: Fence Erectors
description: Erect and repair metal and wooden fences and fence gates around highways, industrial establishments, residences, or farms.

title: Hazardous Materials Removal Workers
description: Identify, remove, pack, transport, or dispose of hazardous materials, including asbestos, lead-based paint, waste oil, fuel, transmission fluid, radioactive materials, or contaminated soil.

title: Highway Maintenance Workers
description: Maintain highways, municipal and rural roads, airport runways, and rights-of-way. Duties include patching broken or eroded pavement and repairing guard rails, highway markers, and snow fences.

title: Rail-Track Laying and Maintenance Equipment Operators
description: Operate equipment to lay, repair, or maintain rail tracks. Includes operating machines that automatically fasten ties and rails together.

title: Septic Tank Servicers and Sewer Pipe Cleaners
description: Clean and repair septic tanks, sewer lines, or drains. May patch walls and partitions of tank, replace damaged drain tile, or repair breaks in underground piping.

title: Segmental Pavers
description: Layout, cut, and place segmental paving units, such as interlocking concrete pavers or bricks. Includes grading and sand leveling.

title: Derrick Operators, Oil and Gas
description: Derrick Operators work on oil and gas drilling rigs, operating and maintaining the derrick structure and hoisting equipment used to lower and raise drill strings and casings. They monitor drilling fluid systems, mix drilling muds, and assist in the handling of drilling tools and equipment during well operations. Their technical proficiency and safety awareness are critical to efficient and safe drilling operations.

title: Rotary Drill Operators, Oil and Gas
description: Rotary Drill Operators control and operate rotary drilling equipment to bore wells for oil and gas extraction. They monitor drilling parameters, adjust equipment settings, and coordinate with the drilling crew to ensure safe and efficient penetration through geological formations. Their expertise in drilling mechanics and well control procedures is fundamental to successful well operations.

title: Service Unit Operators, Oil and Gas
description: Service Unit Operators perform well servicing and workover operations on oil and gas wells to restore or enhance production. They operate specialized equipment to clean, repair, and stimulate wells, and assist in the installation and removal of downhole equipment. Their technical skills support the ongoing productivity and maintenance of oil and gas assets.

title: Excavating and Loading Machine and Dragline Operators, Surface Mining
description: Excavating and Loading Machine and Dragline Operators use large earthmoving and material-handling equipment to remove overburden and extract minerals at surface mining sites. They operate draglines, power shovels, and excavators to load material onto haul trucks and maintain pit faces according to mine plans. Their equipment proficiency and spatial awareness are essential to efficient and safe surface mining operations.

title: Earth Drillers, Except Oil and Gas
description: Earth Drillers operate drilling equipment to bore holes in the ground for purposes such as water well installation, geotechnical investigation, and environmental monitoring. They set up and operate rotary, percussion, or auger drilling rigs, collect soil and rock samples, and maintain equipment in field conditions. Their technical knowledge of drilling methods and subsurface conditions supports a variety of construction and environmental projects.

title: Explosives Workers, Ordnance Handling Experts, and Blasters
description: Explosives Workers, Ordnance Handling Experts, and Blasters place and detonate explosive charges to fracture rock and earth in mining, construction, and demolition operations. They plan blast designs, prepare and handle explosive materials safely, and comply with strict regulatory requirements governing the storage and use of explosives. Their expertise ensures controlled and effective blasting while protecting personnel and surrounding infrastructure.

title: Continuous Mining Machine Operators
description: Continuous Mining Machine Operators control automated cutting machines that extract coal and other minerals from underground seams in a single continuous operation. They maneuver large machines to cut and load material onto conveyor systems, monitor equipment performance, and respond quickly to mechanical or safety issues. Their precision and underground awareness are critical to safe and productive mining operations.

title: Roof Bolters, Mining
description: Roof Bolters operate specialized drilling and bolting equipment to install roof support systems in underground mines, preventing cave-ins and ensuring safe working conditions. They drill holes into mine roofs and walls, insert and secure bolts and mesh, and inspect supported areas for stability. Their work is a critical safety function in underground mining environments.

title: Loading and Moving Machine Operators, Underground Mining
description: Loading and Moving Machine Operators in underground mining use scoops, shuttle cars, and other equipment to transport extracted ore and material from mining faces to haulage systems. They operate in confined underground environments, maintaining equipment and coordinating with mining crews to ensure efficient material flow. Their equipment skills and situational awareness are vital to underground mining productivity and safety.

title: Rock Splitters, Quarry
description: Rock Splitters use hand tools, wedges, and mechanical equipment to separate large blocks of stone from quarry walls and faces for use in construction and manufacturing. They drill holes, insert splitting devices, and apply controlled force to fracture rock along desired planes with precision. Their skill in reading rock structure and applying appropriate splitting techniques minimizes waste and produces usable stone.

title: Roustabouts, Oil and Gas
description: Roustabouts perform general labor and maintenance tasks on oil and gas drilling rigs and production facilities, including cleaning, painting, moving equipment, and assisting skilled workers. They help assemble and disassemble drilling equipment, maintain rig site cleanliness, and perform routine upkeep tasks under the direction of more experienced crew members. Their physical labor and reliability are foundational to smooth oilfield operations.

title: Helpers--Extraction Workers
description: Extraction Worker Helpers assist skilled extraction workers in mining, quarrying, and oil and gas operations by performing manual and semi-skilled support tasks. They carry tools and materials, clean and maintain equipment, and assist with the setup and operation of extraction machinery under close supervision. Their physical support enables extraction crews to work safely and efficiently.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers
description: First-Line Supervisors of Mechanics, Installers, and Repairers oversee teams of maintenance and repair workers to ensure that equipment installations, servicing, and repairs are completed safely, correctly, and on schedule. They assign work orders, monitor quality and productivity, troubleshoot complex technical problems, and ensure compliance with safety standards. Their technical expertise and leadership are essential to maintaining operational reliability.

title: Computer, Automated Teller, and Office Machine Repairers
description: Computer, Automated Teller, and Office Machine Repairers install, maintain, and repair computers, ATMs, printers, and other office equipment for businesses and organizations. They diagnose hardware and software faults, replace defective components, and perform preventive maintenance to minimize downtime. Their technical skills ensure the reliable operation of essential business and financial technology.

title: Radio, Cellular, and Tower Equipment Installers and Repairers
description: Radio, Cellular, and Tower Equipment Installers and Repairers install, test, and maintain communications equipment on towers, rooftops, and in facilities to support cellular and radio networks. They climb and work at heights to mount antennas and transceivers, run cabling, and troubleshoot signal and connectivity issues. Their technical expertise keeps wireless communications infrastructure operational.

title: Telecommunications Equipment Installers and Repairers, Except Line Installers
description: Telecommunications Equipment Installers and Repairers set up, configure, and maintain equipment such as switches, routers, and PBX systems used in telephone and data networks. They troubleshoot malfunctions, replace defective components, and ensure systems are optimized for reliable communication service delivery. Their technical knowledge supports the infrastructure of modern voice and data communications.

title: Avionics Technicians
description: Avionics Technicians install, inspect, test, adjust, and repair avionics equipment including navigation, communications, and flight control systems on aircraft. They use diagnostic software and precision test instruments to identify faults and ensure airworthiness compliance. Their meticulous work is critical to aviation safety and the reliable performance of complex aircraft systems.

title: Electric Motor, Power Tool, and Related Repairers
description: Electric Motor, Power Tool, and Related Repairers diagnose and repair electric motors, generators, transformers, and power tools used in industrial and commercial settings. They rewind coils, replace bearings and brushes, and test repaired equipment to verify proper function and electrical safety. Their expertise extends the service life of electrical equipment and reduces operational downtime.

title: Electrical and Electronics Installers and Repairers, Transportation Equipment
description: These technicians install, maintain, and repair electrical and electronic systems in transportation vehicles including trains, aircraft, ships, and military equipment. They diagnose electrical faults using schematics and diagnostic tools, replace defective components, and verify system functionality after repair. Their specialized knowledge of transportation electrical systems is critical to the safety and reliability of vehicles.

title: Electrical and Electronics Repairers, Commercial and Industrial Equipment
description: Electrical and Electronics Repairers for commercial and industrial equipment diagnose, repair, and maintain the electrical and electronic systems of manufacturing machinery, industrial controls, and commercial appliances. They use test equipment to isolate faults, replace components, and recalibrate systems to restore full operational performance. Their technical expertise minimizes production downtime and ensures equipment safety.

title: Electrical and Electronics Repairers, Powerhouse, Substation, and Relay
description: Powerhouse, Substation, and Relay Repairers install, maintain, and repair the high-voltage electrical equipment found in power generation plants and electrical substations. They test and calibrate protective relay systems, inspect transformers and switchgear, and troubleshoot faults that could compromise the stability of power transmission networks. Their precision and adherence to strict safety protocols are essential to grid reliability.

title: Electronic Equipment Installers and Repairers, Motor Vehicles
description: Electronic Equipment Installers and Repairers for motor vehicles install and repair electronic systems such as audio, navigation, security, and telematics equipment in cars, trucks, and commercial vehicles. They integrate aftermarket and factory systems, diagnose electronic faults, and ensure proper wiring and system configuration. Their work combines automotive knowledge with electronics expertise.

title: Audiovisual Equipment Installers and Repairers
description: Audiovisual Equipment Installers and Repairers set up, configure, and maintain AV systems including projectors, displays, sound systems, and video conferencing technology in commercial, educational, and entertainment environments. They troubleshoot signal and connectivity issues, replace faulty components, and train users on equipment operation. Their technical knowledge ensures reliable and high-quality AV performance.

title: Security and Fire Alarm Systems Installers
description: Security and Fire Alarm Systems Installers mount, wire, and configure security and fire detection systems in residential and commercial buildings. They install components such as sensors, panels, cameras, and communication devices, test systems for proper operation, and train occupants on system use. Their work protects lives and property through reliable installation of life-safety and security technology.

title: Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians
description: Aircraft Mechanics and Service Technicians inspect, maintain, and repair aircraft airframes, engines, and systems to ensure continued airworthiness. They perform scheduled and unscheduled maintenance, diagnose mechanical and electrical defects, and complete required documentation in compliance with FAA regulations. Their rigorous attention to safety and technical standards is fundamental to aviation safety.

title: Automotive Body and Related Repairers
description: Automotive Body and Related Repairers restore the structural integrity and appearance of vehicles damaged by collision, corrosion, or other causes. They straighten frames, repair or replace body panels, apply fillers, and prepare surfaces for refinishing to return vehicles to pre-damage condition. Their craftsmanship and knowledge of automotive construction ensure safe and aesthetically correct repairs.

title: Automotive Glass Installers and Repairers
description: Automotive Glass Installers and Repairers replace or repair cracked and broken windshields and other vehicle glass using specialized tools and adhesive systems. They remove damaged glass, prepare surfaces, install new units to manufacturer specifications, and perform resin injection repairs on minor chips and cracks. Their precise work ensures structural integrity and driver visibility.

title: Automotive Service Technicians and Mechanics
description: Automotive Service Technicians and Mechanics inspect, diagnose, and repair mechanical, electrical, and electronic systems in cars and light trucks. They perform routine maintenance such as oil changes and brake services, as well as complex repairs to engines, transmissions, and computer-controlled systems. Their diagnostic skills and familiarity with modern vehicle technology are central to keeping vehicles safe and roadworthy.

title: Bus and Truck Mechanics and Diesel Engine Specialists
description: Bus and Truck Mechanics and Diesel Engine Specialists maintain and repair heavy-duty vehicles including commercial trucks, buses, and diesel-powered equipment. They inspect and service diesel engines, braking systems, transmissions, and electrical components, and diagnose issues using computer diagnostics and manual testing. Their expertise keeps commercial fleets safe, compliant, and operational.

title: Farm Equipment Mechanics and Service Technicians
description: Farm Equipment Mechanics and Service Technicians maintain and repair agricultural machinery such as tractors, combines, and planting equipment. They diagnose mechanical, hydraulic, and electronic faults, perform seasonal servicing, and conduct field repairs to minimize agricultural downtime. Their knowledge of precision agriculture technology and mechanical systems is increasingly important in modern farming operations.

title: Mobile Heavy Equipment Mechanics, Except Engines
description: Mobile Heavy Equipment Mechanics maintain and repair the hydraulic, pneumatic, and mechanical systems of construction and industrial machinery such as bulldozers, cranes, and excavators. They inspect and service undercarriages, hydraulic cylinders, and drive systems, and diagnose faults using onboard diagnostic systems. Their technical expertise ensures the reliable operation of high-value construction assets.

title: Rail Car Repairers
description: Rail Car Repairers inspect, maintain, and repair railroad freight and passenger cars, including their wheels, brakes, couplings, and structural components. They conduct safety inspections, perform welding and fabrication work, and replace worn or defective parts to ensure rail cars meet Federal Railroad Administration standards. Their thorough maintenance work is critical to the safety and reliability of rail transport.

title: Motorboat Mechanics and Service Technicians
description: Motorboat Mechanics and Service Technicians inspect, maintain, and repair inboard and outboard engines, drive systems, and related marine equipment on recreational and commercial watercraft. They diagnose mechanical and electrical faults, perform seasonal maintenance, and test repaired vessels on water to verify proper operation. Their expertise ensures the safety and performance of marine vessels.

title: Motorcycle Mechanics
description: Motorcycle Mechanics diagnose, maintain, and repair motorcycles, scooters, and related two-wheeled vehicles, servicing engines, drivetrains, brakes, and electrical systems. They perform routine maintenance services and complex repairs, using manufacturer-specific diagnostic tools and technical manuals. Their technical skill and passion for powersports equipment ensure reliable and safe vehicle operation.

title: Outdoor Power Equipment and Other Small Engine Mechanics
description: Outdoor Power Equipment and Small Engine Mechanics service and repair lawn mowers, chainsaws, generators, and other equipment powered by small gasoline engines. They diagnose engine, carburetor, ignition, and mechanical faults, perform tune-ups and part replacements, and test equipment after repair. Their knowledge of small engine systems supports a broad range of residential and commercial customers.

title: Bicycle Repairers
description: Bicycle Repairers assemble, adjust, and repair bicycles, including their gearing, braking, wheel, and frame systems. They diagnose mechanical issues, replace worn components, and perform custom builds and upgrades for recreational and competitive cyclists. Their technical craftsmanship and product knowledge support a growing cycling community.

title: Recreational Vehicle Service Technicians
description: Recreational Vehicle Service Technicians inspect, maintain, and repair motorhomes, travel trailers, and campers, servicing their mechanical, electrical, plumbing, and structural systems. They diagnose and repair propane, 12-volt, and shore power systems, as well as slideout mechanisms and appliances specific to the RV industry. Their broad technical skills across multiple systems are essential to keeping RVs safe and road-ready.

title: Tire Repairers and Changers
description: Tire Repairers and Changers mount, balance, and repair tires on passenger vehicles, trucks, and other equipment in automotive service centers and tire shops. They use specialized machinery to remove and install tires, patch punctures, and check and adjust inflation pressures to manufacturer specifications. Their efficient and accurate work is essential to vehicle safety and customer turnaround.

title: Mechanical Door Repairers
description: Mechanical Door Repairers install, service, and repair automatic and manual commercial doors, garage doors, and related hardware. They diagnose mechanical and electrical faults in door systems, replace worn components such as springs, cables, and operators, and perform preventive maintenance to ensure safe and reliable door operation. Their work spans commercial, industrial, and residential service calls.

title: Control and Valve Installers and Repairers, Except Mechanical Door
description: Control and Valve Installers and Repairers install, maintain, and repair mechanical regulating and controlling devices such as thermostats, pressure gauges, and safety valves used in industrial and utility systems. They calibrate instruments, troubleshoot control malfunctions, and ensure that regulatory and process control equipment operates within specified parameters. Their precision maintenance supports safe and efficient industrial process operations.

title: Heating, Air Conditioning, and Refrigeration Mechanics and Installers
description: HVACR Mechanics and Installers install, maintain, and repair heating, ventilation, air conditioning, and refrigeration systems in residential and commercial buildings. They diagnose mechanical and electrical faults, charge refrigerant systems, and perform seasonal tune-ups to ensure energy-efficient and reliable climate control. Their work supports occupant comfort and compliance with environmental regulations governing refrigerants.

title: Home Appliance Repairers
description: Home Appliance Repairers diagnose and fix household appliances such as washers, dryers, refrigerators, ovens, and dishwashers at customer homes or in service centers. They use diagnostic tools and technical manuals to identify faults, order and install replacement parts, and test repaired appliances for proper function. Their technical skill and customer service orientation support efficient and reliable appliance maintenance.

title: Industrial Machinery Mechanics
description: Industrial Machinery Mechanics maintain and repair factory production equipment, automated assembly systems, and heavy industrial machinery to minimize downtime and ensure manufacturing efficiency. They diagnose mechanical, hydraulic, pneumatic, and electrical faults, replace worn components, and conduct preventive maintenance on complex production systems. Their expertise is critical to maintaining the continuous operation of manufacturing facilities.

title: Maintenance Workers, Machinery
description: Machinery Maintenance Workers lubricate, clean, and perform routine upkeep on industrial and manufacturing equipment to prevent breakdowns and extend service life. They replace filters, belts, and other consumable parts, monitor equipment condition, and report potential issues to maintenance mechanics or supervisors. Their consistent preventive care reduces costly unplanned downtime in production environments.

title: Millwrights
description: Millwrights install, maintain, and dismantle industrial machinery and mechanical equipment including turbines, conveyors, pumps, and production systems in manufacturing and industrial facilities. They use precision alignment tools and rigging equipment to position heavy components, and perform detailed mechanical adjustments to ensure optimal equipment performance. Their specialized skills are in high demand during plant construction, expansion, and major overhauls.

title: Refractory Materials Repairers, Except Brickmasons
description: Refractory Materials Repairers apply heat-resistant materials to furnaces, boilers, kilns, and other high-temperature industrial equipment to protect against heat damage and maintain operational efficiency. They mix and apply refractory cements, castables, and ceramic fibers, and inspect linings for wear or damage requiring repair. Their specialized knowledge of refractory materials supports industries operating extreme-temperature processes.

title: Electrical Power-Line Installers and Repairers
description: Electrical Power-Line Installers and Repairers construct, maintain, and repair overhead and underground electrical transmission and distribution lines. They climb poles and towers, string conductors, install transformers and switching equipment, and respond to emergency outages to restore power. Their work at height and with high-voltage systems demands rigorous safety training and physical capability.

title: Telecommunications Line Installers and Repairers
description: Telecommunications Line Installers and Repairers set up and maintain the network of cables and related infrastructure that delivers telephone, internet, and cable television services to customers. They climb poles, work underground, and install aerial and buried lines, splicing and testing cables to ensure signal quality and connectivity. Their field work is foundational to accessible telecommunications infrastructure.

title: Camera and Photographic Equipment Repairers
description: Camera and Photographic Equipment Repairers diagnose and repair mechanical, optical, and electronic faults in cameras, lenses, and related photographic equipment. They disassemble and clean optical assemblies, replace worn shutters and aperture mechanisms, and recalibrate autofocus and metering systems. Their precise technical skill preserves the function of high-value imaging equipment.

title: Medical Equipment Repairers
description: Medical Equipment Repairers install, inspect, calibrate, and repair complex medical devices and diagnostic equipment used in healthcare facilities. They follow manufacturer service manuals and regulatory standards to maintain imaging systems, patient monitors, infusion pumps, and other critical healthcare technology. Their work ensures that medical equipment is safe, accurate, and available for patient care.

title: Musical Instrument Repairers and Tuners
description: Musical Instrument Repairers and Tuners restore and maintain the playability and tonal quality of musical instruments including pianos, brass, woodwinds, and stringed instruments. They diagnose mechanical and acoustic issues, replace pads and strings, regulate action, and perform precision tuning to restore instruments to optimal playing condition. Their craft combines technical skill with a strong musical ear.

title: Watch and Clock Repairers
description: Watch and Clock Repairers diagnose and restore the mechanical and electronic movements of timepieces ranging from wristwatches to antique clocks. They disassemble movements, clean and lubricate components, replace worn parts, and reassemble and regulate timepieces to precise accuracy standards. Their meticulous craftsmanship preserves functional and heirloom timepieces.

title: Maintenance and Repair Workers, General
description: General Maintenance and Repair Workers perform a wide variety of building and equipment maintenance tasks including painting, plumbing, electrical repairs, and carpentry in residential and commercial properties. They respond to work orders, conduct inspections, and complete repairs across multiple trades to keep facilities safe and functional. Their broad skill set makes them versatile contributors to property management operations.

title: Wind Turbine Service Technicians
description: Wind Turbine Service Technicians inspect, maintain, and repair wind turbines and their mechanical, electrical, and hydraulic systems to ensure efficient and safe energy generation. They climb turbine towers to access nacelles, diagnose faults using SCADA systems and diagnostic tools, and perform scheduled and unscheduled maintenance. Their technical expertise and comfort working at heights are essential to the renewable energy sector.

title: Coin, Vending, and Amusement Machine Servicers and Repairers
description: Coin, Vending, and Amusement Machine Servicers and Repairers maintain and repair automated vending machines, arcade games, and coin-operated equipment at customer locations. They restock products, collect cash, diagnose mechanical and electronic faults, and replace defective parts to keep machines operational and profitable. Their technical versatility and time management support large territories of managed machines.

title: Commercial Divers
description: Commercial Divers perform underwater work including construction, inspection, repair, and salvage operations for offshore oil and gas, marine construction, and public utility clients. They use scuba and surface-supplied diving equipment to complete tasks such as welding, cutting, and non-destructive testing at depth. Their combination of diving expertise and trade skills enables critical work in challenging underwater environments.

title: Locksmiths and Safe Repairers
description: Locksmiths and Safe Repairers install, adjust, and repair locking mechanisms, safes, and access control systems for residential, commercial, and automotive clients. They respond to emergency lockouts, rekey or replace locks, and install high-security hardware to protect property and improve security. Their expertise in lock mechanisms and security systems is foundational to physical security services.

title: Manufactured Building and Mobile Home Installers
description: Manufactured Building and Mobile Home Installers set up and anchor prefabricated homes and modular buildings on prepared sites. They connect utility systems, install skirting, and ensure that structures are level, secure, and compliant with installation codes and manufacturer specifications. Their work transforms factory-built housing components into ready-to-occupy residences.

title: Riggers
description: Riggers attach cables, chains, and rigging hardware to heavy equipment and structural loads to facilitate safe lifting and movement by cranes and hoisting systems. They inspect rigging equipment, calculate load weights and center of gravity, and direct crane operators during complex lifts on construction and industrial sites. Their expertise in load management and rigging safety prevents accidents involving heavy materials.

title: Signal and Track Switch Repairers
description: Signal and Track Switch Repairers install and maintain the electrical signaling and track switching systems that direct and control train movements along rail networks. They test signal circuits, replace defective components, and ensure that switching and signal equipment meets safety and operational standards. Their meticulous maintenance work is critical to the safe and efficient movement of rail traffic.

title: Helpers--Installation, Maintenance, and Repair Workers
description: Installation, Maintenance, and Repair Helpers assist skilled tradespeople by carrying tools and materials, holding components in place, and performing basic maintenance tasks under close supervision. They support electricians, plumbers, HVAC technicians, and other tradespeople to increase crew efficiency and productivity on job sites. Their entry-level role provides foundational exposure to skilled trades and a pathway to apprenticeship.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Production and Operating Workers
description: First-Line Supervisors of Production and Operating Workers directly oversee factory and production workers, ensuring that manufacturing operations meet safety, quality, and output targets. They assign and monitor work, enforce safety protocols, resolve production issues, and provide performance feedback to their teams. Their hands-on leadership is essential to maintaining productivity and quality on the production floor.

title: Aircraft Structure, Surfaces, Rigging, and Systems Assemblers
description: Aircraft Structure, Surfaces, Rigging, and Systems Assemblers fabricate and assemble the structural components, control surfaces, and mechanical systems of aircraft according to engineering specifications and quality standards. They use precision tools to drill, fasten, and fit airframe parts, install control cables and hydraulic lines, and inspect completed assemblies for compliance with aerospace standards. Their craftsmanship is foundational to aircraft airworthiness.

title: Coil Winders, Tapers, and Finishers
description: Coil Winders, Tapers, and Finishers wind wire coils used in electromagnetic devices such as motors, transformers, and solenoids, and apply insulating tape and finishes to completed coils. They operate manual and automated winding machines, follow winding specifications, and inspect coils for dimensional accuracy and electrical integrity. Their precision work is essential to the performance of electromagnetic components.

title: Electrical and Electronic Equipment Assemblers
description: Electrical and Electronic Equipment Assemblers build and assemble electrical and electronic products such as circuit boards, control panels, and power distribution equipment from component parts. They follow assembly diagrams and work instructions, solder connections, and test completed assemblies for proper electrical function. Their precision assembly skills ensure the quality and reliability of finished electrical products.

title: Electromechanical Equipment Assemblers
description: Electromechanical Equipment Assemblers assemble products that combine electrical and mechanical components such as sensors, actuators, and automated devices. They read assembly drawings, fit mechanical parts, make electrical connections, and test assembled units for proper function and quality compliance. Their interdisciplinary assembly skills support manufacturing in industries such as automotive, aerospace, and industrial automation.

title: Engine and Other Machine Assemblers
description: Engine and Other Machine Assemblers fabricate and assemble complex engines, turbines, and industrial machinery from precision-machined parts. They follow detailed assembly instructions, use precision measurement tools to verify tolerances, and perform functional tests on completed assemblies. Their exacting work ensures that high-performance mechanical systems meet design specifications and quality standards.

title: Structural Metal Fabricators and Fitters
description: Structural Metal Fabricators and Fitters cut, shape, and assemble metal components to create structural frameworks for buildings, ships, bridges, and industrial equipment. They read fabrication drawings, use cutting and forming equipment, and fit and tack weld assemblies in preparation for final welding. Their craftsmanship provides the structural steel foundations for major construction and manufacturing projects.

title: Fiberglass Laminators and Fabricators
description: Fiberglass Laminators and Fabricators apply layers of fiberglass and resin to molds to produce composite parts and products for boats, automotive components, and construction applications. They prepare molds, lay up fiberglass reinforcement, apply resins, and finish cured parts by trimming and sanding. Their knowledge of composite materials and lamination techniques ensures structural strength and surface quality in finished products.

title: Timing Device Assemblers and Adjusters
description: Timing Device Assemblers and Adjusters assemble and calibrate precision timing mechanisms for clocks, watches, and other instruments requiring accurate timekeeping. They work under magnification with extremely small components, following detailed assembly procedures and using precision instruments to test and regulate completed movements. Their meticulous craftsmanship is essential to the accuracy and reliability of timekeeping devices.

title: Team Assemblers
description: Team Assemblers work as part of production crews to assemble manufactured products on assembly lines, rotating between workstations and tasks as needed. They follow standardized work instructions, use hand and power tools to complete assembly steps, and inspect their work for quality compliance. Their flexibility and teamwork enable efficient, high-volume manufacturing across a wide range of industries.

title: Bakers
description: Bakers prepare and produce breads, pastries, and other baked goods using ovens and baking equipment in commercial bakeries, restaurants, and retail settings. They measure and mix ingredients according to recipes, shape and proof doughs, and monitor baking times and temperatures to achieve consistent quality. Their craftsmanship and attention to consistency deliver products that meet customer expectations and food safety standards.

title: Butchers and Meat Cutters
description: Butchers and Meat Cutters prepare and portion meat products for retail and foodservice customers, breaking down whole carcasses into primal and retail cuts using knives, saws, and other equipment. They trim excess fat, grind meat, and package products in accordance with food safety and customer specifications. Their knife skills and knowledge of meat anatomy ensure safe, efficient, and waste-minimizing processing.

title: Meat, Poultry, and Fish Cutters and Trimmers
description: Meat, Poultry, and Fish Cutters and Trimmers perform repetitive cutting, trimming, and boning tasks on production lines in meatpacking and food processing plants. They use knives and mechanical equipment to remove bones, excess fat, and connective tissue from animal products in high-speed, high-volume processing environments. Their speed, consistency, and adherence to food safety standards are essential to efficient production.

title: Slaughterers and Meat Packers
description: Slaughterers and Meat Packers work in meat processing facilities performing tasks related to the humane slaughter of livestock and the initial processing and packing of carcasses. They follow strict food safety, animal welfare, and hygiene protocols to ensure that products meet regulatory standards and quality requirements. Their work in physically demanding and regulated environments requires strict compliance with safety and sanitation procedures.

title: Food and Tobacco Roasting, Baking, and Drying Machine Operators and Tenders
description: Food and Tobacco Roasting, Baking, and Drying Machine Operators and Tenders set up and operate industrial ovens, dryers, and roasters used in large-scale food and tobacco processing. They monitor temperature, humidity, and timing parameters, adjust equipment settings, and inspect output for quality compliance throughout production runs. Their equipment vigilance ensures consistent product quality and food safety in high-volume processing environments.

title: Food Batchmakers
description: Food Batchmakers operate equipment and follow formulations to mix, blend, and cook ingredients in the production of processed food products such as sauces, soups, and confections. They measure and combine ingredients according to recipes, control cooking parameters, and ensure that batches meet quality and food safety standards before packaging. Their attention to recipe compliance and process consistency is essential to uniform product quality.

title: Food Cooking Machine Operators and Tenders
description: Food Cooking Machine Operators and Tenders set up and monitor automated cooking equipment such as retorts, fryers, and continuous ovens used in commercial food processing facilities. They adjust cooking controls, observe product flow, and sample output to verify that products meet specified cooking and quality requirements. Their vigilance and knowledge of food processing equipment support safe and efficient high-volume food production.

title: Extruding and Drawing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to extrude or draw thermoplastic or metal materials into tubes, rods, hoses, wire, bars, or structural shapes.

title: Forging Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend forging machines to taper, shape, or form metal or plastic parts.

title: Rolling Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to roll steel or plastic forming bends, beads, knurls, rolls, or plate or to flatten, temper, or reduce gauge of material.

title: Cutting, Punching, and Press Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to saw, cut, shear, slit, punch, crimp, notch, bend, or straighten metal or plastic material.

title: Drilling and Boring Machine Tool Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend drilling machines to drill, bore, ream, mill, or countersink metal or plastic work pieces.

title: Grinding, Lapping, Polishing, and Buffing Machine Tool Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend grinding and related tools that remove excess material or burrs from surfaces, sharpen edges or corners, or buff, hone, or polish metal or plastic work pieces.

title: Lathe and Turning Machine Tool Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend lathe and turning machines to turn, bore, thread, form, or face metal or plastic materials, such as wire, rod, or bar stock.

title: Milling and Planing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend milling or planing machines to mill, plane, shape, groove, or profile metal or plastic work pieces.

title: Machinists
description: Set up and operate a variety of machine tools to produce precision parts and instruments. Includes precision instrument makers who fabricate, modify, or repair mechanical instruments.

title: Metal-Refining Furnace Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend furnaces, such as gas, oil, coal, electric-arc or oxygen furnaces, to melt and refine metal before casting or to produce specified types of steel.

title: Pourers and Casters, Metal
description: Operate hand-controlled mechanisms to pour and regulate the flow of molten metal into molds to produce castings or ingots.

title: Model Makers, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up and operate machines to make working models of metal or plastic objects.

title: Patternmakers, Metal and Plastic
description: Lay out, machine, fit, and assemble castings and parts to metal or plastic foundry patterns, core boxes, or match plates.

title: Foundry Mold and Coremakers
description: Make or form wax or sand cores or molds used in the production of metal castings in foundries.

title: Molding, Coremaking, and Casting Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend metal or plastic molding, casting, or coremaking machines to mold or cast metal or thermoplastic parts or products.

title: Multiple Machine Tool Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend more than one type of cutting or forming machine tool or robot.

title: Tool and Die Makers
description: Analyze specifications, lay out metal stock, set up and operate machine tools, and fit and assemble parts to make and repair dies, cutting tools, jigs, fixtures, gauges, and machinists' hand tools.

title: Welders, Cutters, Solderers, and Brazers
description: Use hand-welding, flame-cutting, hand-soldering, or brazing equipment to weld or join metal components or to fill holes, indentations, or seams of fabricated metal products.

title: Welding, Soldering, and Brazing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend welding, soldering, or brazing machines or robots that weld, braze, solder, or heat treat metal products, components, or assemblies.

title: Heat Treating Equipment Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend heating equipment, such as heat-treating furnaces, flame-hardening machines, induction machines, soaking pits, or vacuum equipment to temper, harden, anneal, or heat treat metal or plastic objects.

title: Layout Workers, Metal and Plastic
description: Lay out reference points and dimensions on metal or plastic stock or workpieces, such as sheets, plates, tubes, structural shapes, castings, or machine parts, for further processing.

title: Plating Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Metal and Plastic
description: Set up, operate, or tend plating or coating machines to coat metal or plastic products with chromium, zinc, copper, precious metals, or other materials to protect or decorate surfaces.

title: Tool Grinders, Filers, and Sharpeners
description: Perform precision smoothing, sharpening, polishing, or grinding of metal objects.

title: Prepress Technicians and Workers
description: Set up and prepare material for printing presses. Includes prepress functions, such as compositing, typesetting, layout, paste-up, formatting, and plate making.

title: Printing Press Operators
description: Set up and operate digital, letterpress, lithographic, flexographic, gravure, or other printing machines.

title: Print Binding and Finishing Workers
description: Bind books and other publications or finish printed products by hand or machine. May set up binding and finishing machines.

title: Laundry and Dry-Cleaning Workers
description: Operate or tend washing or dry-cleaning machines to wash or dry-clean industrial or household articles, such as cloth garments, suede, leather, furs, blankets, draperies, linens, rugs, and carpets.

title: Pressers, Textile, Garment, and Related Materials
description: Press or shape articles by hand or machine.

title: Sewing Machine Operators
description: Operate or tend sewing machines to join, reinforce, decorate, or perform related sewing operations in the manufacture of garment or nongarment products.

title: Shoe and Leather Workers and Repairers
description: Construct, decorate, or repair leather and leather-like products, such as luggage, shoes, and saddles.

title: Shoe Machine Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend a variety of machines to join, decorate, reinforce, or finish shoes and shoe parts.

title: Sewers, Hand
description: Sew, join, reinforce, or finish, usually with needle and thread, a variety of manufactured items.

title: Tailors, Dressmakers, and Custom Sewers
description: Design, make, alter, repair, or fit garments.

title: Textile Bleaching and Dyeing Machine Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend machines to bleach, shrink, wash, dye, or finish textiles or synthetic or glass fibers.

title: Textile Cutting Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut textiles.

title: Textile Knitting and Weaving Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that knit, loop, weave, or draw in textiles.

title: Textile Winding, Twisting, and Drawing Out Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that wind or twist textiles; or draw out and combine sliver, such as wool, hemp, or synthetic fibers.

title: Extruding and Forming Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Synthetic and Glass Fibers
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that extrude and form continuous filaments from synthetic materials, such as liquid polymer, rayon, and fiberglass.

title: Fabric and Apparel Patternmakers
description: Draw and construct sets of precision master fabric patterns or layouts. May also mark and cut fabrics and apparel.

title: Upholsterers
description: Make, repair, or replace upholstery for household furniture or transportation vehicles.

title: Cabinetmakers and Bench Carpenters
description: Cut, shape, and assemble wooden articles or set up and operate a variety of woodworking machines, such as power saws, jointers, and mortisers to surface, cut, or shape lumber or to fabricate parts for wood products.

title: Furniture Finishers
description: Shape, finish, and refinish damaged, worn, or used furniture or new high-grade furniture to specified color or finish.

title: Model Makers, Wood
description: Construct full-size and scale wooden precision models of products.

title: Patternmakers, Wood
description: Plan, lay out, and construct wooden unit or sectional patterns used in forming sand molds for castings.

title: Sawing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Wood
description: Set up, operate, or tend wood sawing machines.

title: Woodworking Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders, Except Sawing
description: Set up, operate, or tend woodworking machines, such as drill presses, lathes, shapers, routers, sanders, planers, and wood nailing machines.

title: Nuclear Power Reactor Operators
description: Operate or control nuclear reactors. Move control rods, start and stop equipment, monitor and adjust controls, and record data in logs.

title: Power Distributors and Dispatchers
description: Coordinate, regulate, or distribute electricity or steam.

title: Power Plant Operators
description: Control, operate, or maintain machinery to generate electric power. Includes auxiliary equipment operators.

title: Stationary Engineers and Boiler Operators
description: Operate or maintain stationary engines, boilers, or other mechanical equipment to provide utilities for buildings or industrial processes.

title: Water and Wastewater Treatment Plant and System Operators
description: Operate or control an entire process or system of machines, often through the use of control boards, to transfer or treat water or wastewater.

title: Chemical Plant and System Operators
description: Control or operate an entire chemical process or system of machines.

title: Gas Plant Operators
description: Distribute or process gas for utility companies and others by controlling compressors to maintain specified pressures on main pipelines.

title: Petroleum Pump System Operators, Refinery Operators, and Gaugers
description: Operate or control petroleum refining or processing units. May specialize in controlling manifold and pumping systems, gauging or testing oil in storage tanks, or regulating the flow of oil in pipelines.

title: Chemical Equipment Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend equipment to control chemical changes or reactions in the processing of industrial or consumer products.

title: Separating, Filtering, Clarifying, Precipitating, and Still Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend continuous flow or vat-type equipment; filter presses; shaker screens; centrifuges; condenser tubes; precipitating, fermenting, or evaporating tanks; scrubbing towers; or batch stills.

title: Crushing, Grinding, and Polishing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to crush, grind, or polish materials, such as coal, glass, grain, stone, food, or rubber.

title: Grinding and Polishing Workers, Hand
description: Grind, sand, or polish, using hand tools or hand-held power tools, a variety of metal, wood, stone, clay, plastic, or glass objects.

title: Mixing and Blending Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to mix or blend materials, such as chemicals, tobacco, liquids, color pigments, or explosive ingredients.

title: Cutters and Trimmers, Hand
description: Use hand tools or hand-held power tools to cut and trim a variety of manufactured items, such as carpet, fabric, stone, glass, or rubber.

title: Cutting and Slicing Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines that cut or slice materials, such as glass, stone, cork, rubber, tobacco, food, paper, or insulating material.

title: Extruding, Forming, Pressing, and Compacting Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines, such as glass forming machines, plodder machines, and tuber machines, to shape and form products.

title: Furnace, Kiln, Oven, Drier, and Kettle Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend heating equipment other than basic metal, plastic, or food processing equipment.

title: Inspectors, Testers, Sorters, Samplers, and Weighers
description: Inspect, test, sort, sample, or weigh nonagricultural raw materials or processed, machined, fabricated, or assembled parts or products for defects, wear, and deviations from specifications.

title: Jewelers and Precious Stone and Metal Workers
description: Design, fabricate, adjust, repair, or appraise jewelry, gold, silver, other precious metals, or gems.

title: Gem and Diamond Workers
description: Fabricate, finish, or evaluate gems and diamonds used in jewelry or industrial tools.

title: Dental Laboratory Technicians
description: Construct and repair full or partial dentures or dental appliances.

title: Medical Appliance Technicians
description: Construct, fit, maintain, or repair medical supportive devices, such as braces, orthotics and prosthetic devices, joints, arch supports, and other surgical and medical appliances.

title: Ophthalmic Laboratory Technicians
description: Cut, grind, and polish eyeglasses, contact lenses, or other precision optical elements.

title: Packaging and Filling Machine Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend machines to prepare industrial or consumer products for storage or shipment.

title: Painting, Coating, and Decorating Workers
description: Paint, coat, or decorate articles such as furniture, glass, pottery, toys, and books.

title: Coating, Painting, and Spraying Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend machines to coat or paint any of a wide variety of products, including glassware, cloth, ceramics, metal, plastic, paper, or wood.

title: Semiconductor Processing Technicians
description: Perform any or all functions in the manufacture of semiconductor devices, such as wafers, integrated circuits, or microchips.

title: Photographic Process Workers and Processing Machine Operators
description: Perform precision work involved in photographic processing, such as editing photographic negatives and prints. Operate photographic processing machines.

title: Computer Numerically Controlled Tool Operators
description: Operate computer-controlled tools, machines, or robots to machine or process parts, tools, or other work pieces made of metal, plastic, wood, stone, or other materials.

title: Computer Numerically Controlled Tool Programmers
description: Develop programs to control machining or processing of metal or plastic parts by automatic machine tools, equipment, or systems.

title: Adhesive Bonding Machine Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend bonding machines that use adhesives to join items for further processing or to form a completed product.

title: Cleaning, Washing, and Metal Pickling Equipment Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend machines to wash or clean products, such as barrels or kegs, glass items, tin plate, food, pulp, coal, plastic, or rubber, to remove impurities.

title: Cooling and Freezing Equipment Operators and Tenders
description: Operate or tend equipment, such as cooling and freezing units, refrigerators, batch freezers, and freezing tunnels, to cool or freeze products.

title: Etchers and Engravers
description: Engrave or etch metal, wood, rubber, or other materials for identification or decorative purposes.

title: Molders, Shapers, and Casters, Except Metal and Plastic
description: Mold, shape, form, cast, or carve products such as food products, pacemakers, brick, tile, candles, and stone by hand or machine.

title: Stone Cutters and Carvers, Manufacturing
description: Cut or carve stone according to diagrams and patterns for use in construction, monument, or building industries.

title: Glass Blowers, Molders, Benders, and Finishers
description: Shape molten glass according to patterns using blowpipes and hand tools to create artistic or industrial glass products.

title: Potters, Manufacturing
description: Operate machines or use hand tools to mold or shape clay and other materials into pottery and ceramics.

title: Paper Goods Machine Setters, Operators, and Tenders
description: Set up, operate, or tend paper goods machines that perform a variety of functions, such as converting, sawing, corrugating, banding, wrapping, boxing, stitching, forming, or sealing paper or paperboard sheets into products.

title: Tire Builders
description: Operate machines to build tires from rubber components.

title: Helpers--Production Workers
description: Help production workers by performing duties requiring less skill. Duties include supplying or holding materials or tools, and cleaning work area and equipment.

title: Aircraft Cargo Handling Supervisors
description: Direct ground crew in the loading, unloading, securing, and staging of aircraft cargo or baggage.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Helpers, Laborers, and Material Movers, Hand
description: Supervise and coordinate the activities of helpers, laborers, or material movers.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Material-Moving Machine and Vehicle Operators
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of material-moving machine and vehicle operators and helpers.

title: First-Line Supervisors of Passenger Attendants
description: Supervise and coordinate activities of passenger attendants on trains, planes, ships, or buses.

title: Airline Pilots, Copilots, and Flight Engineers
description: Pilot and navigate the flight of fixed-wing aircraft on scheduled air carrier routes for the transport of passengers and cargo.

title: Commercial Pilots
description: Pilot and navigate the flight of fixed-wing aircraft on nonscheduled air carrier routes, or helicopters. Includes charter pilots, corporate pilots, and aerial crop dusters.

title: Air Traffic Controllers
description: Control air traffic on and within vicinity of airport and movement of air traffic between altitude sectors and control centers according to established procedures and policies.

title: Airfield Operations Specialists
description: Ensure the safe takeoff and landing of commercial and military aircraft. Duties include coordination between air-traffic control and maintenance personnel.

title: Flight Attendants
description: Provide personal services to ensure the safety, security, and comfort of airline passengers during flight.

title: Ambulance Drivers and Attendants, Except Emergency Medical Technicians
description: Drive ambulance or assist ambulance driver in transporting sick, injured, or convalescent persons. Assist in lifting patients.

title: Driver/Sales Workers
description: Drive truck or other vehicle over established routes or within an established territory and sell or deliver goods, such as food products, including restaurant take-out items, or pick up or deliver items such as commercial laundry.

title: Heavy and Tractor-Trailer Truck Drivers
description: Drive a tractor-trailer combination or a truck with a capacity of at least 26,001 pounds Gross Vehicle Weight (GVW). May be required to unload truck.

title: Light Truck Drivers
